title: Quadratic Unet for seismic random noise attenuation
authors: Xiaojing Wang, Yuhan Sui, Jianwei Ma
abstract: In the field of seismic data processing, seismic denoising is essential to improve the quality of seismic data. Various deep learning methods have been proposed, showing promising performance for seismic denoising. However, most deep learning methods are based on linear neurons, resulting in limited expressive ability for complex seismic signals. To enhance denoising capability using non-linear neurons, the authors propose a supervised quadratic U-shaped network (QUnet) for seismic random noise attenuation. The quadratic neurons in QUnet are represented by a second-order polynomial of input data and weighted parameters. Numerical synthetic seismic data experiments show that the proposed QUnet method achieves higher signal-to-noise ratio results and preserves the continuity of reflectors more effectively.

title: Posterior sampling for random noise attenuation via score-based generative models
authors: Chuangji Meng, Jinghuai Gao, Baohai Wu, Hongling Chen, Yajun Tian
abstract: This paper introduces a posterior sampling framework for random noise attenuation via score-based generative models (SGMPS-RNA). The method leverages score-based generative models to sample from the posterior distribution of clean seismic data given noisy observations, enabling effective random noise suppression without requiring paired clean-noisy training data.

title: Enhancing seismic noise suppression using the Noise2Noise framework
authors: Mitsuyuki Ozawa
abstract: This research introduces the Noise2Noise Enhancement (N2NE) framework, which improves seismic noise reduction capabilities without requiring clean reference signals. The key contributions include: 1) Repeated-shot scenarios: The N2NE framework enhances conventional vertical stacking using Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) trained on repeated-shot pairs. A substack strategy further boosts performance. 2) Non-repeated-shot scenarios: The Cross-domain N2NE framework refines conventional denoising methods by utilizing information from both common-shot and receiver domains.

title: Fast diffusion model for seismic data noise attenuation
authors: Junheng Peng, Yong Li, Yingtian Liu, Mingwei Wang, Zhangquan Liao, Xiaowen Wang
abstract: Strong noise typically interferes with seismic exploration. Under such conditions, most existing methods cannot achieve satisfactory results. Recently, methods based on diffusion models have been applied to process the strong noise in seismic data. However, the computational efficiency of diffusion-based methods is considerably lower than that of conventional methods. To address this issue, we develop a diffusion model based on the Bayesian equation and deep learning and improve its reverse equation for higher efficiency. In addition, we develop a new normalization method and an adaptive method for estimating relevant parameters. Through various improvements, our method achieves significantly better noise attenuation performance than the benchmark methods on synthetic and field data sets, while also achieving a several-fold increase in computational speed.

title: Beyond linear: Hybrid neuron network for coherent noise suppression
authors: Meng Wang, Xiaotong Li, Jianwei Ma
abstract: Seismic data denoising is a crucial step in seismic exploration. Deep learning has emerged as a powerful tool for suppressing coherent noise in seismic data. Although most studies focus on designing complex network architectures, we investigate the benefits of neuronal diversity from a neuronal perspective. First, we introduce the construction of quadratic neurons and demonstrate that they achieve a faster convergence rate than traditional linear neurons. Based on this, we develop a hybrid neuron (HN) block that integrates linear and quadratic neurons into a unified module, leveraging the strengths of both. Using the HN block, we build an HN network (HN-Net). We apply HN-Net to suppress coherent seismic noise and compare its performance with DnCNN, U-Net, and f-k filtering on synthetic and field data.

title: A diffusion-hybrid framework with deformable convolution and multihead self-attention for seismic denoising
authors: Mingwei Wang, Yong Li, Yingtian Liu, Junheng Peng, Huating Li, Xiaowen Wang
abstract: Although diffusion models demonstrate robust noise attenuation capabilities, noise residue and geologic structure distortion may occur in complex geologic environments. To mitigate these limitations, we develop a hybrid denoising framework, which integrates diffusion models with deformable convolutions and multihead self-attention (MSA) mechanisms. First, we integrate MSA mechanisms into the diffusion architecture to improve model performance. Second, the integration of deformable convolutions empowers the network to enhance the accuracy of local structural feature extraction by dynamically adjusting the sampling positions of convolution kernels. The final framework achieves global progressive denoising through diffusion processes while performing local structural refinement.

title: Robust seismic data denoising via self-supervised deep learning
authors: Ji Li, Daniel Trad, Dawei Liu
abstract: Seismic data denoising is a critical component of seismic data processing, yet effectively removing erratic noise, characterized by its non-Gaussian distribution and high amplitude, remains a substantial challenge for conventional methods and deep-learning (DL) algorithms. Supervised learning frameworks typically outperform others, but they require pairs of noisy data sets alongside corresponding clean ground truth, which is impractical for real-world seismic data sets. This paper introduces a novel zero-shot unsupervised DL framework designed specifically to mitigate random and erratic noise, with a particular emphasis on blended noise. Drawing inspiration from Noise2Noise (N2N) and data augmentation principles, we develop a robust self-supervised denoising network called robust Noiser2Noiser.

title: Diffusion models for multidimensional seismic noise attenuation and superresolution
authors: Yuan Xiao, Kewen Li, Yimin Dou, Wentao Li, Zhixuan Yang, Xinyuan Zhu
abstract: Seismic data quality proves pivotal to its interpretation, necessitating the reduction of noise and enhancement of resolution. Traditional and deep-learning-based solutions have achieved varying degrees of success on low-dimensional seismic data. We develop a deep generative solution for high-dimensional seismic data denoising and superresolution through the innovative application of denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs), which we refer to as MD Diffusion. MD Diffusion treats degraded seismic data as a conditional prior that guides the generative process, enhancing the capability to recover data from complex noise. By iteratively training an implicit probability model, we achieve a sampling speed 10 times faster than the original DDPM.

title: Ground-truth-free deep learning for 3D seismic denoising and reconstruction with channel attention mechanism
authors: Yang Cui, Juan Wu, Min Bai, Yangkang Chen
abstract: Seismic denoising methods using supervised training normally rely on a large number of high-quality paired training data sets to obtain satisfactory results. There are two ways to generate labels for network training: one is to create synthetic data using the wave equation and the other is to use denoised data obtained through prior denoisers. However, using either training data generation methods will limit the networks' denoising performances when faced with complex field data. Here, we develop a ground-truth-free method for 3D seismic data denoising. To improve its denoising efficiency, we exploit efficient channel attention and a convolutional block attention module to adjust the response of different channels to capture their correlation and significance using only a few parameters.

title: Integrating self-supervised denoising in inversion-based seismic deblending
authors: Nick Luiken, Matteo Ravasi, Claire Birnie
abstract: To limit the time, cost, and environmental impact associated with the acquisition of seismic data, considerable effort has been put into simultaneous shooting acquisitions, where seismic sources are fired at short time intervals between each other. As a consequence, waves originating from consecutive shots are entangled within the seismic recordings, yielding blended data. For processing and imaging purposes, the data generated by each individual shot must be retrieved. This process, called deblending, is achieved by solving an inverse problem that is heavily underdetermined. In this work, the authors introduce a new concept that consists of embedding a self-supervised denoising network into the plug-and-play (PnP) framework.

title: Removing abnormal environmental noise in nodal land seismic data using deep learning
authors: Xiaobin Li, Qiaomu Qi, Yuyong Yang, Pengfei Duan, Zhonglin Cao
abstract: In field seismic data, abnormal environmental noise from the acquisition environment often is unavoidable, and its characteristics are complex. Abnormal environmental noise has a strong amplitude (tens of thousands times the average seismic amplitudes) and masks the effective seismic signal. The traditional method for removing such abnormal environmental noise relies on energy scanning to identify the noise location. However, energy scanning cannot always accurately identify such noise due to the sudden energy change at the locations of first-breaks and surface waves. A deep learning method for removing abnormal environmental noise has been developed.

title: Residual learning with feedback for strong random noise attenuation in seismic data
authors: Z. Liao, Y. Li, Y. Liu, Y. Yang, Y. Zhang
abstract: This paper extends residual learning concepts to seismic data denoising, specifically addressing strong random noise attenuation. The feedback mechanism in their approach refers to a recursive or iterative refinement strategy where denoised outputs are fed back into the network to further improve noise suppression—particularly effective for scenarios with high noise levels where standard residual learning may struggle.

title: Unsupervised seismic random noise attenuation by a recursive deep image prior
authors: Y. Zhang, B. Wang
abstract: This paper presents an unsupervised deep learning approach for seismic random noise attenuation without requiring clean training labels. It uses the Deep Image Prior (DIP) framework in a recursive/recurrent architecture specifically adapted for seismic data. The method eliminates the need for paired noisy-clean training data and leverages the implicit prior of convolutional neural network structures.

title: Accelerating seismic scattered noise attenuation in offset-vector tile domain: Application of deep learning
authors: Dawei Liu, Xiaokai Wang, Xiaohai Yang, Haibo Mao, Mauricio D. Sacchi
abstract: This paper focuses on scattered noise attenuation in prestack seismic data, which is computationally intensive in the offset-vector tile (OVT) domain. The authors apply deep learning techniques to accelerate the noise attenuation process in the OVT domain, using deep learning to speed up scattered noise attenuation while maintaining data quality.

title: Adapting the residual dense network for seismic data denoising and upscaling
authors: Rongqian Wang, Ruixuan Zhang, Chenglong Bao, Lingyun Qiu, Dinghui Yang
abstract: This paper presents a supervised deep learning approach using the Residual Dense Network (RDN) architecture adapted for seismic data processing. The method addresses three main tasks: random noise suppression, missing trace interpolation, and super-resolution/upscaling. The approach consists of a three-step pipeline: denoising, interpolation, and super-resolution.

title: Deep unfolding dictionary learning for seismic denoising
authors: Yuhan Sui, Xiaojing Wang, Jianwei Ma
abstract: Seismic denoising is an essential step for seismic data processing. Conventionally, dictionary learning (DL) methods for seismic denoising always assume the representation coefficients to be sparse and the dictionary to be normalized or a tight frame. Current DL methods need to update the dictionary and the coefficients in an alternating iterative process. However, the dictionary obtained from the DL method often needs to be recalculated for different input data. We have developed a novel and interpretable deep unfolding dictionary learning (DUDL) method for seismic denoising by unfolding the iterative algorithm of DL into a deep neural network (DNN).

title: DL²: Dictionary learning regularized with deep learning prior for simultaneous denoising and interpolation
authors: Lina Liu, Jianwei Ma
abstract: The dictionary learning method has been successfully applied to denoise and interpolate seismic data. However, this method cannot be used to adequately interpret weak seismic events and structural features. By combining dictionary learning and a convolutional neural network (CNN) denoiser, we constructed a new dictionary learning method regularized by a supervised denoiser (DL-SD). In addition to the sparse prior used in previous dictionary learning, the CNN denoiser learns from sizeable amounts of natural images using a deep neural network to help regularize the fine and structural features of data in the DL-SD.

title: Retrieving useful signals from highly corrupted erratic noise using robust residual dictionary learning
authors: Wei Chen, Yapo Abolé Serge Innocent Oboué, Yangkang Chen
abstract: Seismic denoising will inevitably cause signal leakage, which is seen as coherent signal energy in the removed noise profile. Most traditional methods either ignore the signal leakage while maintaining clean denoised data or adjust parameters to decrease the signal leakage while causing more residual noise in the denoised data. The local signal-and-noise orthogonalization method can help retrieve the leaked signals without bringing significant residual noise. However, when seismic data contain erratic noise, the local orthogonalization method does not work properly because of the unstable inversion. We propose a robust dictionary learning and sparse coding algorithm to retrieve the leaked signals.

title: Seismic data recovery using deep targeted denoising priors in an alternating optimization framework
authors: Nanying Lan, Fanchang Zhang
abstract: This paper presents a method for seismic data recovery that combines deep targeted denoising priors with an alternating optimization framework. The method leverages deep learning-based denoising priors specifically targeted for seismic data characteristics. An alternating optimization framework is used to solve the seismic data recovery (interpolation/reconstruction) problem. The approach integrates deep neural network denoisers as regularization priors within a classical optimization scheme.

title: Simple framework for the contrastive learning of visual representations-based data-driven tight frame for seismic denoising and interpolation
authors: Jinghe Li, Xiangling Wu
abstract: This paper combines SimCLR (A Simple Framework for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations) with data-driven tight frame methods for seismic data processing. The method applies contrastive learning principles to develop a data-driven tight frame approach specifically for seismic denoising and interpolation tasks.

title: Erratic and random noise attenuation using adaptive local orthogonalization
authors: Yapo Abole Serge Ismael Oboué, Yangkang Chen, Min Bai, Wenchao Chen
abstract: The paper proposes an adaptive local orthogonalization algorithm to attenuate high-amplitude erratic noise and high-frequency random noise. The method combines hard thresholding operator and exponential moving-average filter. This approach addresses limitations of conventional denoising frameworks, particularly for erratic noise that follows non-Gaussian distributions and has high amplitude.

title: Complete and representative training of neural networks A generalization study using double noise injection and natural images
authors: Chao Zhang, Mirko van der Baan
abstract: Neural networks hold substantial promise to automate various processing and interpretation tasks. Yet their performance is often sub-optimal compared with standard but more closely guided approaches. Lack of performance is often attributed to poor generalization, in particular if fewer training examples are provided than free parameters exist in the machine learning algorithm. Network generalization is improved if the provided samples are representative, in that they describe all features of interest well. We illustrate that one possible solution is to make the problem more general if this greatly increases the number of available training data.

title: Dictionary learning with convolutional structure for seismic data denoising and interpolation
authors: Murad Almadani, Umair bin Waheed, Mudassir Masood, Yangkang Chen
abstract: Seismic data inevitably suffers from random noise and missing traces in field acquisition. This limits the utilization of seismic data for subsequent imaging or inversion applications. Recently, dictionary learning has gained remarkable success in seismic data denoising and interpolation. We test the use of convolutional sparse coding (CSC) model for seismic data denoising and interpolation. In particular, we use the local block coordinate descent (LoBCoD) algorithm to reconstruct missing traces and clean seismic data from noisy input.

title: Intelligent random noise modeling by the improved variational autoencoding method and its application to data augmentation
authors: Qiankun Feng, Yue Li, Hongzhou Wang
abstract: This paper presents an improved variational autoencoding (VAE) method for intelligent modeling of random noise in geophysical data and its application to data augmentation for geophysical datasets.

title: Deep denoising autoencoder for seismic random noise attenuation
authors: Omar M. Saad, Yangkang Chen
abstract: This paper presents a deep denoising autoencoder (DDAE) approach for attenuating random noise in seismic data. The method uses a deep learning-based unsupervised/semi-supervised approach for seismic random noise attenuation. It leverages autoencoder architecture to learn meaningful representations of clean seismic signals from noisy data.

title: Deep learning for denoising
authors: Siwei Yu, Jianwei Ma, Wenlong Wang
abstract: Compared with traditional seismic noise attenuation algorithms that depend on signal models and their corresponding prior assumptions, removing noise with a deep neural network is trained based on a large training set, where the inputs are the raw datasets and the corresponding outputs are the desired clean data. After the completion of training, the deep learning method achieves adaptive denoising with no requirements of accurate modelings of the signal and noise, or optimal parameters tuning. We call this intelligent denoising. We use a convolutional neural network (CNN) as the basic tool for deep learning.

title: Dictionary learning based on dip patch selection training for random noise attenuation
authors: Shaohuan Zu, Hui Zhou, Rushan Wu, Maocai Jiang, Yangkang Chen
abstract: We have developed a dip-oriented dictionary learning method, which incorporates an estimation of the dip field into the selection procedure of training patches, and applies a curvelet-transform noise reduction method to remove some fine-scale components that presumably contain mostly random noise.

title: White noise attenuation of seismic trace by integrating variational mode decomposition with convolutional neural network
authors: H. Wu, B. Zhang, T. Lin, F. Li, N. Liu
abstract: This paper presents a method that combines variational mode decomposition (VMD) with convolutional neural network (CNN) for white noise attenuation in seismic traces. The approach integrates traditional signal processing techniques with deep learning for seismic data denoising.

title: Seismic Random Noise Attenuation Using Non-Local Reference-Guided Deep Image Prior
authors: N. Liu, Z. Wang, G. Hua, W. Deng, J. Gao
abstract: This study proposes a non-local reference-guided deep image prior (DIP) framework for seismic random noise attenuation. It extends non-local self-similarity from the patch to pixel level to improve noise estimation and generate structurally consistent reference data, introduces a noise-driven early stopping criterion for unsupervised optimization, and applies selective weight decay to enhance signal-noise separation without requiring clean training data. Experiments demonstrate effective noise suppression while preserving structural continuity and reflection characteristics.

title: Seismic Random Noise Attenuation via ChannelAttention-Weighted Unsupervised Deep Learning
authors: Yuting Wang, Hongsun Fu, Ruixue Gu, Nan Zhao
abstract: We propose an encoder-decoder architecture that uses a channel-attention mechanism to suppress random noise in seismic data. Our autoencoder structure consists of three parts: an encoder module, a decoder module, and an attention skip connection module, which is a type of unsupervised deep learning model that uses the patching technique. Both encoder and decoder modules use fully connected layers, which allow for comprehensive information integration and feature extraction.

title: Removing random noise and improving the resolution of seismic data using deep-learning transformers
authors: Q. Sun, Y. Feng, Q. Du, F. Gong
abstract: The paper proposes Seis-SUnet, a deep learning approach that achieves simultaneous random noise suppression and super-resolution reconstruction of post-stack seismic data. Key contributions include: Conv-Swin-Block Design that combines ordinary convolution with Swin transformer to capture long-distance dependencies in seismic data spatial locations; Hybrid Training Strategy using a combination of L1 loss, Edge loss, and Multi-scale structural similarity loss.

title: Re-visible blind block network: An unsupervised seismic datarandom noise attenuation method
authors: Jing Wang, Bangyu Wu, Hui Yang, Bo Li
abstract: The paper proposes a novel one-stage unsupervised denoising framework called the Re-visible Blind Block Network for seismic data random noise attenuation. The method only requires a single noisy seismic dataset, eliminating the need for clean labels or extensive training datasets. It uses two complementary block-wise masks to mask noisy data, and introduces a re-visible loss to improve denoising performance.

title: Random noise attenuation of ocean bottom seismometers basedon a substep deep denoising autoencoder
authors: Haoran Lin, Jian Xu, Lei Xing, Qianqian Li, Huaishan Liu
abstract: Ocean bottom seismometer data usually contain a large amount of random noise, which seriously reduces the signal-to-noise ratio of the data and affects subsequent imaging. A novel approach is proposed to attenuate the marine seismic random noise of ocean bottom seismometers based on a six-dense-layer denoising autoencoder. The method uses a substep (pretraining) strategy: pretrain the encoder based on clean synthetic seismic data and pretrain the decoder based on noisy synthetic seismic data, then fine-tune the pretrained model on raw seismic data in an unsupervised manner.

title: Robust fast dictionary learning for seismic noise attenuation
authors: Z. Feng
abstract: This paper proposes a robust fast dictionary learning method for seismic noise attenuation. The method improves upon conventional dictionary learning by introducing robustness to erratic noise and faster computation.

title: Unsupervised dual learning for seismic data denoising in the absence of labelled data
authors: Yu Xing Zhao, Yue Li, Ning Wu
abstract: The training and updating of the supervised deep learning model are both dependent on labelled data. Unfortunately, the data-labelling process is usually expensive and time consuming. This paper introduces an unpaired domain-to-domain translation method based on the framework of two-way generative adversarial networks (CycleGAN), which does not need to use labelled data for training. We use an unpaired mixed training set containing synthetic seismic data and real seismic data to train the network.

title: Seismic noise attenuation by signal reconstruction: an unsupervised machine learning approach
authors: Y. Gao, F. Zhao, G. Li, H. Li
abstract: The paper presents an unsupervised machine learning approach for seismic noise attenuation based on signal reconstruction. The method uses deep convolutional neural networks in an unsupervised learning manner, reconstructs seismic signals without requiring clean training labels or synthetic noise-free data, and uses only raw noisy data as input with the network learning hidden features directly from the noisy input.

title: Two-dimensional dictionary learning for suppressing random seismic noise
authors: Kunhong Li, Zhining Liu, Bin She, Hanpeng Cai, Yaojun Wang, Guangmin Hu
abstract: Dictionary learning is a successful method for random seismic noise attenuation. The local patches extracted from the seismic section are essentially two-dimensional matrices. However, almost all existing dictionary learning methods convert each two-dimensional patch into a one-dimensional vector, destroying the geometric structure information. In this paper, we propose a two-dimensional dictionary learning method for the seismic denoising problem that represents the two-dimensional patches directly to avoid the conversion process and thus reserves the important structure information for a better reconstruction.

title: U-STDRNet: A unified model integrating swin transformer and residual dense network for seismic image super-resolution and denoising
authors: Mingliao Wu, Juan Wu, Min Bai, Haiyu Li, Zhixian Gui, Guangtan Huang
abstract: Enhancing seismic image resolution while effectively suppressing noise remains a critical challenge. This study proposes a deep learning-based joint model, U-Net Shifted Window (Swin) Transformer-based dense residual network (U-STDRNet). The model integrates the global modeling capability of the Swin Transformer, the hierarchical feature reuse mechanism of the residual dense network, and an attention-guided strategy to jointly perform seismic image super-resolution and denoising.

title: Multi-stage progressive network for seismic random noise suppression
authors: Guanghui Li, Huiwei Li, Shoufeng He, Li Wang
abstract: Seismic data quality frequently deteriorates due to random noise contamination. The proposed multi-stage progressive U-shaped convolutional network (MPU-Net) architecture addresses limitations of conventional single-stage architectures through supervised cross-stage attention mechanisms that maintain feature connectivity throughout the network. Group enhanced convolutional blocks (GEB)-MPU-Net introduces GEB to specifically counteract the progressive attenuation of shallow features in deep networks.

title: Seismic signal denoising using variational mode decomposition and a denoising convolutional neural network
authors: Shengrong Zhang, Liang Zhang, Xuesha Qin
abstract: Effectively recovering signals buried in noise remains a challenging topic in seismic data denoising. This study proposed an effective method called variational mode decomposition (VMD)-denoising convolutional neural network (DnCNN). The method first applies VMD to decompose the originally noisy signal into multiple intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) with band-pass characteristics, thereby achieving effective decoupling of different frequency components and noise separation. Selected IMFs are then combined into a multi-channel input and fed into the DnCNN for end-to-end modeling and denoising reconstruction.

title: Self-guided attention denoising network for pre-stack seismic data: From coarse to fine
authors: Xintong Dong, Jun Lin, Shaoping Lu, Ming Cheng, Hongzhou Wang
abstract: Background noise attenuation is one of the most essential steps in seismic data processing. We propose a novel architecture: self-guided attention network (SGA-Net) by combining self-guided strategy and spatial attention mechanism. Different from most conventional CNNs, this proposed SGA-Net can capture multi-scale features by performing the convolution operation on seismic data with different resolutions. The self-guided strategy is adopted to take full advantage of the multi-scale features.

title: Seismic random noise suppression using denoising autoencoder
authors: Hui Song, Menghua Fang, Cheng Zhou, Houqiang Gao
abstract: In the seismic data acquisition phase, random noise will inevitably be introduced as undesirable components. In this article, we propose a denoising framework based on denoising autoencoder (DAE) for seismic random noise suppression. DAE can directly reconstruct noise-free seismic data from noisy seismic data in an unsupervised learning manner. The entire seismic data reconstruction requires three phases: corrupting phase, encoding phase, and decoding phase.

title: Seismic random noise attenuation using multi-scale sparse dictionary learning
authors: Jinwei Fang, Liang Zhang, Hui Zhou, Shengdong Liu, Bo Wang, Wenjie Chen
abstract: Seismic data contain random noise, which affects data processing and interpretation. To effectively remove such noise, we develop a denoising workflow based on multi-scale sparse dictionary learning. The multi-scale sparse dictionary learning method decreases the complexity of data by two approaches: seismic data-sparse representation from the data domain to the wavelet domain by wavelet bases, and denoising by sparse dictionary learning in a certain frequency band without the noise effect from other frequency bands.

title: Desert seismic data denoising and effective signal recovery by using improved shearlet transform based on the deep-learning coefficient selection
authors: Xintong Dong, Yue Li, Baojun Yang
abstract: Contamination of seismic data by background noise causes difficulties for imaging, reservoir fluid prediction, and stratigraphic interpretation. Desert seismic data poses a particular problem mainly due to low signal-to-noise ratio and serious frequency spectrum overlapping between the effective signals and low-frequency noise. We utilize the convolutional neural network (CNN) to act as a threshold function, thereby establishing an optimal non-linear relationship between noisy coefficients and effective signal coefficients. We take shearlet transform as an example to verify the effectiveness of the proposed CNN-based threshold function.

title: Seismic data denoising using double sparsity dictionary and alternating direction method of multipliers
authors: Liang Zhang, Liguo Han, Ao Chang, Jinwei Fang, Pan Zhang, Yong Hu, Zhengguang Liu
abstract: Recently, dictionary learning plays a more and more important role in seismic data denoising. However, dictionary learning often produces artifacts due to no prior-constraint structural information. In this paper, we propose a new denoising approach, which has double sparsity and combines the advantage of fixed-basis transform and dictionary learning. The alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) is used in sparse coding to update dictionary coefficients, and the data-driven tight frame (DDTF) is used in dictionary updating to update dictionary atoms.

title: Low-frequency noise suppression for desert seismic data based on a wide inference network
authors: Yue Li, Wentao Yu, Chao Zhang, Baojun Yang
abstract: This work presents a wide inference network (WIN) approach for suppressing low-frequency noise in desert seismic data. The research addresses a significant challenge in seismic exploration: desert environments produce strong low-frequency noise that contaminates seismic signals and degrades data quality. The wide inference network was developed to effectively attenuate low-frequency noise specific to desert seismic environments while preserving useful seismic signal components.

title: Seismic signal denoising using total generalized variation with overlapping group sparsity in the accelerated ADMM framework
authors: Yingpin Chen, Zhenming Peng, Meihui Li, Fei Yu, Fan Lin
abstract: The total generalized variation (TGV) denoising model is the extension of the total variation (TV) model and is capable of avoiding the staircase artifacts of the TV model. However, the TGV model independently disposes the pixels, ignoring the structural similarity prior. By introducing the overlapping group sparsity to the TGV model, a new improved TGV model is proposed, exploring both the first-order and second-order neighborhood differential gradient information to improve the robustness of the TGV to heavy noise pollution. To solve the proposed model, we adopted the accelerated alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM).

title: Combined multi-branch selective kernel hybrid-pooling skip connection residual network for seismic random noise attenuation
authors: Meng Zeng, Gulan Zhang, Yong Li, Yiliang Luo, Guanghui Hu, Yanlin Huang, Chenxi Liang
abstract: To improve the generalization ability of the single pooling skip connection residual network for seismic random noise attenuation, we present a hybrid-pooling skip connection residual network (HSN). To further improve the network performance, we propose a combined multi-branch selective kernel (CSK) hybrid-pooling skip connection residual network (CHSN). In CHSN, CSK consists of a three-branch selective kernel (TSK) and our suggested four-branch selective kernel (FSK), and aims to adaptively capture feature maps for high-accuracy effective information recovery.

title: Intelligent data-driven denoising based on texture complexity
authors: Jingye Li, Xiaohong Liu, Yang Liu, Zhenhua Huo
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent data-driven denoising method that adapts to local texture complexity in geophysical data. The approach uses texture complexity analysis to guide the denoising process, allowing for adaptive preservation of structural features while removing noise.

title: Seismic profile denoising based on common-reflection-point gathers using convolution neural networks
authors: Shuaishuai Li, Jiangjie Zhang, Qian Cheng, Feng Zhu, Linong Liu
abstract: With the development of seismic surveys and the decline of shallow petroleum resources, high resolution and high signal-to-noise ratio have become more important in seismic processing. We have developed CNNs for noise reduction based on common-reflection-point (CRP) gathers. We used CRP gathers of stationary-phase migration as labels and CRP gathers of conventional prestack time migration as inputs. The results showed that our methods can achieve fast and reliable denoising and produce high-quality stack profiles that contain true amplitude information.

title: Seismic random noise attenuation using DnCNN with stratigraphic dip constraint
authors: Wei Yang, Xuehua Chen, Ying Rao
abstract: A method for seismic random noise detection and suppression using Denoising Convolutional Neural Network (DnCNN) is presented, and non-Gaussian noise reduction technology is used to achieve blind noise reduction at unknown levels. The paper introduces a DnCNN-based approach with a stratigraphic dip constraint, which addresses non-Gaussian noise, enables blind denoising, and incorporates stratigraphic dip information as a constraint to better preserve meaningful seismic events while removing noise.

title: Unsupervised deep learning seismic data random noise attenuation with early stopping
authors: Zitai Xu, Bangyu Wu
abstract: Suppression of seismic random noise is one critical step in seismic data processing. The unsupervised deep image prior (DIP) model has achieved promising denoising results without training labels. However, during training, these models first learn the effective seismic events in the noisy data, and then pick up the random noise afterwards, i.e. overfitting. Thus, the practicability of DIP hinges on good early stopping (ES) that catches the potentially noise-free seismic data. In this paper, we investigate the ES strategy in seismic data denoising using DIP method, which consistently detects the performance of reconstruction sequence by observing its running variance (VAR). Numerical tests on 2D/3D synthetic and field data demonstrate that compared with other stopping criteria, the ES method exhibits superiority in suppressing random noise and preserves the effective signals better.

title: Regularized deep learning for unsupervised random noise attenuation in poststack seismic data
authors: Chengyun Song, Shutao Guo, Chuanchao Xiong, Jiying Tuo
abstract: The paper addresses a key challenge in deep learning-based seismic denoising: the difficulty of obtaining large amounts of paired noisy-clean training data. The proposed method is fully unsupervised and works as follows: (1) Self-supervised training strategy: The original seismic data is split into odd traces and even traces, which are used as network input and output respectively, enabling training directly on noisy data without clean labels. (2) Two regularization terms to prevent over-smoothing: First regularization term constrains the network toward an ideal denoising network to prevent overfitting; Second regularization term incorporates lateral variation characteristics of seismic data to better preserve structural information.

title: DPN2N: A self-supervised method for seismic data denoising and key information preservation
authors: Kewen Li, Chunlong Li, Yuan Xiao, Yimin Dou, Xinyuan Zhu, Guangyue Zhou
abstract: The focus of this study is on the use of a self-supervised deep learning method called Neighbor2Neighbor for seismic data denoising, aiming to address two key challenges: (1) The high cost of obtaining clean noise-free data; (2) The difficulty in balancing denoising effectiveness with the retention of critical information. Key contributions include: Novel Regularization technique that reduces noise while preserving essential high-frequency information such as faults; Architecture built on the ResUNet++ architecture, tailored to the frequency characteristics of seismic data; Self-Supervised Approach eliminates the need for expensive clean samples by using Neighbor2Neighbor methodology.

title: Seismic data reconstruction via non-uniform Fourier transform and implicit neural network denoising prior
authors: Feiying Wang, Yuhan Sui, Xiaojing Wang, Tong Zhou, Jianwei Ma
abstract: Irregular sampling of seismic data on non-equispaced spatial grids presents significant challenges in seismic data acquisition and imaging, resulting in reduced quality and continuity of subsurface structures. Conventional methods combined the curvelet transform with the non-uniform discrete Fourier transform (NDFT) to reconstruct seismic data but often suffered from spectral leakage and incomplete continuity of seismic events. To address this, we propose an advanced seismic data reconstruction approach that integrates the NDFT with an implicit denoising neural network prior. The proposed method leverages the NDFT to map irregular sampling points accurately to equispaced grids while incorporating the denoising neural network as a prior to suppress noise and enhance the continuity of seismic events.

title: Seismic deblending with self-supervised Unblind-trace denoising
authors: Shirui Wang, Wenyi Hu, Pengyu Yuan, Xuqing Wu, Qunshan Zhang, Prashanth Nadukandi, German Ocampo Botero, Jiefu Chen
abstract: The paper addresses simultaneous-source seismic acquisition (blended acquisition), where multiple seismic sources are fired at short time intervals, causing shot responses to overlap in recorded data. The key contributions include: A novel self-supervised learning method for seismic deblending that does not require labeled training datasets; Uses a blind-trace deep neural network with a carefully crafted blending loss function; Demonstrates accurate separation of individual source-response pairs under three different blended-acquisition designs.

title: Simultaneous suppression of seismic random and erratic noise using PINN with high-frequency preservation
authors: Peihong Xie, Yang Liu, Cai Liu, Chao Song, Xiangjia Zhang
abstract: The random and erratic noise are common in seismic data. Traditional denoising methods that combine noise suppression for both types will lead to error accumulation. Deep learning methods often require large amounts of labeled data and complex network architectures to tackle this problem. To overcome these limitations, we utilize a physics-informed neural network (PINN) to remove both types of noise in this research. This method does not require a large amount of labeled data. Because it uses slope attribute, which is based on a local plane-wave partial differential equation (PDE). We apply velocity-dependent (VD) slope estimation to get more accurate slope values. To enhance its ability of preservation in high-frequency signal, we use Fourier feature embedding and a periodic activation function from sinusoidal representation networks (SIREN).

title: Multi-condition seismic data denoising using feature-expanded gradient penalty generative adversarial network
authors: Xiaotian Xue, Xi Yi, Jingtao Wang, Xu Deng
abstract: To overcome the label scarcity in supervised methods and the blurred outputs of unsupervised approaches for seismic denoising, we propose a semi-supervised gradient-penalized GAN with feature expansion (FEWGAN-SGP) guided by easily-generated pseudo-labels. The network integrates a global residual structure for stable convergence, a channel attention mechanism with feature expansion for fine detail extraction, and a smooth gradient penalty to strengthen discrimination between field and generated images. Experiments on 2D and 3D datasets show that the proposed method achieves superior signal preservation and noise attenuation compared with mainstream traditional and unsupervised approaches.

title: Asymmetric learning based deep denoiser for nonstationary desert seismic noise suppression
authors: Hongbo Lin, Xue Song, Shifu Wang, Haitao Ma, Xuejun Wang
abstract: Random noise in desert seismic data has the weak similarity to the effective signals and spatiotemporally variable noise intensity. Mismatch noise intensity degrades the denoising performance of the convolutional neural network in incomplete noise suppression and damaged seismic signals. We propose an asymmetric learning-based nonstationary convolutional denoising neural network (AL-NCDNet) by introducing an asymmetric loss function and the noise level (NL) estimation block. Since the denoised result is robust to the overestimated NL and more sensitive to the underestimated NL, an asymmetric learning framework is adopted by leveraging a weighted loss function for constraining the noise level estimation block.

title: Deep diffusion models for seismic processing
authors: Ricard Durall, Ammar Ghanim, Mario Ruben Fernandez, Norman Ettrich, Janis Keuper
abstract: Seismic data processing involves techniques to deal with undesired effects that occur during acquisition and pre-processing. These effects mainly comprise coherent artefacts such as multiples, non-coherent signals such as electrical noise, and loss of signal information at the receivers that leads to incomplete traces. Motivated by the limitation that discriminative algorithms lack semantic understanding of the provided data, in this work we employ a generative solution, as it can explicitly model complex data distributions and hence, yield to a better decision-making process. In particular, we introduce diffusion models for three seismic applications: demultiple (multiple removal), denoising, and interpolation.

title: Denoising sparker seismic data with Deep BiLSTM in fractional Fourier transform
authors: Dawoon Lee, Sungryul Shin, Eun-Min Yeo, Wookeen Chung
abstract: Sparkers are widely-used sources of high-resolution images for shallow sub-bottom seismic monitoring. Sparker seismic data contain high random noise levels caused by the high voltage and electrical current required to power the sparker source. Random noise is distributed over the entire frequency and wavenumber, and is thereby difficult to suppress. Numerous studies have been conducted to develop random noise suppression by using convolutional neural networks (CNNs). However, because CNNs learn common characteristics of random noise without considering the sequence while training, learning the sequence relationship between useful signal and random noise in time-series data is difficult. As an alternative, we proposed a recurrent neural network (RNN)-based denoising method that considers seismic data sequences. We proposed a denoising method for sparker seismic data that uses bidirectional long short-term memory RNN in fractional Fourier transform (FrFT).

title: Mutual-guided scale-aggregation denoising network for seismic noise attenuation
authors: Tie Zhong, Zheng Cong, Xunqian Tong, Shiqi Dong, Shaoping Lu, Xintong Dong
abstract: The background noise contained in seismic records contaminate the effective reflection waves and impact the subsequent processes, such as inversion and migration. The properties of seismic noises, such as non-Gaussianity and non-linearity, will be even more complex in challenging exploration environments. Deep-learning techniques are effective in suppressing complex seismic noises and outperform conventional denoising algorithms. Nonetheless, most deep learning networks are designed to extract the features of input data in single-scale only, which leads to inadequate performance when dealing with complicated seismic data. To enhance the denoising capability for seismic noises of deep learning, a novel mutual-guided scale-aggregation denoising network (MSD-Net) is designed to suppress seismic noises by utilizing the multi-scale features of input data.

title: Seismic data denoising with two-step prediction strategy based on Neural Network
authors: Yongjie Zhang, Bingluo Gu, Zhiguang Sun, Xinyue Yan, Shanshan Zhang
abstract: Seismic data denoising (SDD) plays an important role in obtaining high-quality data for subsequent seismic imaging and inversion. However, traditional SDD methods still present several disadvantages such as parameter selection difficulties, high computational costs, and a strong dependence on the experience of processing personnel. Deep learning (DL)-based SDD methods can overcome these issues to a certain extent, having the advantages of high computational efficiency and negligible dependency on the experience of processing personnel. However, existing DL-based SDD methods typically adopt a specific single-network architecture, which can be further improved in terms of training efficiency and denoising quality. In this paper, we propose a two-step prediction denoising method based on the combination of two identical or different network architectures for improving the accuracy and efficiency of DL-based SDD methods.

title: Swin Transformer for simultaneous denoising and interpolation of seismic data
authors: Lei Gao, Housen Shen, Fan Min
abstract: Seismic data are often characterized by low quality due to noise contamination or missing traces. Convolutional neural networks are popular in dealing with denoising and interpolation. However, fixed-size convolution kernels have limited feature extraction range, and popular networks aim at either denoising or interpolating. In this paper, we propose a Swin Transformer convolutional residual network (SCRN) for simultaneous denoising and interpolation. We step-by-step show on synthetic datasets that SCRN can effectively denoise, interpolate, or denoise and interpolate simultaneously. Following this, the trained simultaneous denoising and interpolation model is directly used to handle multi-tasks on the field datasets.

title: A Potential Solution to Insufficient Target-Domain Noise Data: Transfer Learning and Noise Modeling
authors: Xintong Dong, Ming Cheng, Hongzhou Wang, Guanghui Li, Jun Lin, Shaoping Lu
abstract: This paper addresses a critical challenge in deep learning-based seismic data denoising: the difficulty of acquiring sufficient target-domain noise training data that matches the distribution of noise in testing data. The authors propose a novel deep learning paradigm combining: (1) Green-function-based seismic noise modeling to generate large amounts of synthetic noise similar to real seismic noise; (2) Transfer learning using synthetic noise as pretraining data in the source domain; (3) Fine-tuning with limited real target-domain noise data to adapt the model. They also designed a multicascade network architecture with attention mechanism. The proposed method achieves denoising performance comparable to using large amounts of target-domain noise data, reducing dependence on scarce real-world noise samples.

title: A Twice Denoising Autoencoder Framework for Random Seismic Noise Attenuation
authors: Z. Liao, Y. Li, E. Xia, Y. Liu, R. Hu
abstract: The proposed framework (TDAE) consists of a denoising autoencoder (DAE) that attenuates random noise without ground truth, using a vectoring patching technique to reduce time complexity. A data generator develops local correlation (LC) to nonlinear LC to detect and extract signal leakage with noise components suppressed, compensates the extracted signal leakage back to the DAE output in a supersaturated way, and uses DAE again to suppress remaining noise in the new record. The supersaturated compensation of the first signal leakage counteracts the signal leakage of the second DAE noise attenuation, effectively addressing the common dilemma of both signal leaking and noise remaining simultaneously.

title: Attenuation of Seismic Random Noise With Unknown Distribution: A Gaussianization Framework
authors: Chuangji Meng, Jinghuai Gao, Yajun Tian, Liang Zhao, Haoqi Zhao, Zhiqiang Wang
abstract: The proposed method is called SRNGF (Seismic Random Noise Gaussianization Framework). It addresses the challenge of attenuating random noise with unknown distribution in seismic data processing. The framework uses a plug-and-play (PaP) approach that couples a Gaussianization submodule (with learnable parameters that map unknown noise distribution to Gaussian noise) and a Gaussian denoising submodule (can be any existing seismic denoiser). The method uses unsupervised online training on a single field data. It can incorporate both non-deep learning and deep learning denoisers.

title: Automatic Identification of Strong Energy Noise in Seismic Data Based on U-Net
authors: Luping Sun, Xiaodong Zheng, L. Ding, H. Shou, Hao Li
abstract: The paper proposes a U-Net-based deep learning approach to automatically identify strong energy noise in seismic data. The method is designed to identify spatiotemporal variations of strong energy noise in seismic data, which helps in determining more effective denoising thresholds, supporting quality control during field data acquisition, and enabling targeted noise attenuation processing.

title: Deep Nonlocal Regularizer: A Self-Supervised Learning Method for 3-D Seismic Denoising
authors: Zitai Xu, Yisi Luo, Bangyu Wu, Deyu Meng, Yangkang Chen
abstract: The paper proposes the Deep Nonlocal Regularizer (DNLR), a novel self-supervised learning method for 3-D seismic denoising that exploits the nonlocal self-similarity (NSS) of seismic data. The method can be flexibly combined with various hand-crafted regularizers (Total Variation, Nuclear Norm, Correlated TV) and easily plugged into existing self-supervised denoising frameworks like Deep Image Prior (DIP) and Self2Self (S2S). The optimization is solved using the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM).

title: Enhancement of the Seismic Data Resolution Through Q-Compensated Denoising Based on Dictionary Learning
authors: Bo Li, Kai Lin, Xiaotao Wen, Chao Tang, Lei Li
abstract: The paper focuses on Q-compensated denoising for seismic data resolution enhancement using dictionary learning approach for simultaneous denoising and attenuation compensation. The proposed method uses a sparse regularization strategy based on dictionary learning that performs denoising and data compensation tasks in parallel, improving both data resolution and signal-to-noise ratio.

title: Learning to Decouple and Generate Seismic Random Noise via Invertible Neural Network
authors: Chuangji Meng, Jinghuai Gao, Yajun Tian, Zhen Li
abstract: The paper proposes using an Invertible Neural Network (INN) to decouple seismic field data into useful signal and random noise components, and generate quality-controlled synthetic field data with realistic noise characteristics. The method requires only a relatively small labeled dataset of field-useful data pairs for training, and can then generate an infinite number of paired samples by sampling in the latent space.

title: Less Data-Dependent Seismic Noise Suppression Method Based on Transfer Learning With Attention Mechanism
authors: Qiankun Feng, Shigang Wang, Yue Li
abstract: This paper proposes a novel seismic noise suppression method that addresses a key limitation of deep learning approaches: their heavy dependence on large amounts of labeled training data. The authors developed a small-sample transfer learning (TL) architecture that uses a fully pretrained model on source data as a feature extractor, transfers and copies rich features from the extractor to a denoiser for fine-tuning on target data, and introduces a Noise Attention Block (NAB) to regularize representations and reduce discrepancy between different datasets.

title: Random Noise Attenuation Using an Unsupervised Deep Neural Network Method Based on Local Orthogonalization and Ensemble Learning
authors: Kunxi Wang, Tianyue Hu, Binglu Zhao, Shangxu Wang
abstract: Random noise suppression can greatly improve the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of seismic signals. To suppress random seismic noise, we propose an unsupervised deep neural network (UDNN) method based on the local cross-correlation (LCC) loss function and ensemble learning. UDNN with two base learners mainly consists of one input data, two deep neural networks (DNNs), and two output data. The proposed UDNN-LCCEL method takes full advantage of the nonlinear mapping capabilities of DNNs and maps noisy data into effective noise-free signals by minimizing the total loss function. The biggest advantage of our proposed method is that it does not need to use noise-free data as label data.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Denoising Model for Seismic Random Noise Attenuation
authors: Chao Liang, Hongbo Lin, Haitao Ma
abstract: The paper proposes an RL-based seismic denoising (RLSD) algorithm for seismic random noise attenuation by designing a region-adaptive approach. The key innovation is using reinforcement learning to create an adaptive denoising model that can handle varying noise characteristics across different regions of seismic data.

title: S2S-WTV: Seismic Data Noise Attenuation Using Weighted Total Variation Regularized Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Zitai Xu, Yisi Luo, Bingbing Wu, Deyu Meng
abstract: Seismic data often undergoes severe noise due to environmental factors, which seriously affects subsequent applications. Traditional hand-crafted denoisers such as filters and regularizations utilize interpretable domain knowledge to design generalizable denoising techniques, while their representation capacities may be inferior to deep learning denoisers. In this work, we propose a self-supervised method that combines the capacities of deep denoiser and the generalization abilities of hand-crafted regularization for seismic data random noise attenuation. Specifically, we leverage the Self2Self (S2S) learning framework with a trace-wise masking strategy for seismic data denoising by solely using the observed noisy data. Parallelly, we suggest the weighted total variation (WTV) to further capture the horizontal local smooth structure of seismic data.

title: Seismic Random Noise Attenuation Based on M-ResUNet
authors: Jian Gao, Zhenchun Li, Min Zhang
abstract: Suppressing random noise and improving the signal-to-noise ratio of seismic data are of great significance for subsequent high-precision processing. As one of the most popular denoising methods, the algorithms based on deep learning usually utilize the networks poor generalization ability for denoising processing. As a result, such methods usually face the problem of high training set construction costs and high computational costs. In addition, the widely used network UNet's processing of multiscale features is limited to its U-shaped processing path. Therefore, we proposed a denoising method based on unsupervised learning, including a new denoising strategy based on spatial correlation, the corresponding training method, and a new network M-ResUNet.

title: Seismic Random Noise Suppression Model Based on Downsampling and Superresolution
authors: Ziyi Fang, Hongbo Lin, Fuyao Sun, Xue Song, Chao Zhang, Bo Wang
abstract: The paper proposes a novel random noise suppression model that decouples denoising and signal restoration into two separate processes: a Downsampling network that uses nonlinear transformations to separate random noise from effective signals while purifying high-order features, and a Super-resolution (SR) network that expands low-dimensional seismic signal content to restore signal structure. The method introduces a new adversarial loss using gradients between generated and real data to enhance perceptual quality of super-resolution results.

title: Simultaneous Seismic Data Denoising and Reconstruction With Attention-Based Wavelet-Convolutional Neural Network
authors: V.C. Dodda, L. Kuruguntla, A.K. Mandpura, et al.
abstract: The knowledge of hidden resources present inside the Earth layers is vital for the exploration of petroleum and hydrocarbons. However, the recorded seismic data are noisy and incomplete with missing traces that leads to misinterpretation of the Earth layers. In this manuscript, we consider seismic data with Gaussian, non-Gaussian noise distribution, regular, and irregular missing traces. We propose a method for simultaneous noise attenuation and reconstruction of the incomplete seismic data with attention-based wavelet convolutional neural network (AWUN). The wavelet transform is used as pooling layer and inverse wavelet transform (IWT) is used for upsampling layers to avoid information loss. The attention module is used to obtain weights for various feature channels with higher weights assigned to the more significant information.

title: Unsupervised Seismic Footprint Removal With Physical Prior Augmented Deep Autoencoder
authors: Feng Qian, Yuehua Yue, Yu He, Hongtao Yu, Yingjie Zhou, Jinliang Tang, Guangmin Hu
abstract: The paper proposes FR-Net (Footprint Removal Network), an unsupervised deep learning method for seismic acquisition footprint suppression. Core innovation: Unidirectional Total Variation (UTV) model combined with Deep Convolutional Autoencoder (DCAE). The approach is prior-augmented, transforming a purely data-driven model by incorporating physical knowledge about the directional properties of footprint noise. Evaluated on three synthetic and field datasets.

title: Unsupervised Deep Learning for Ground Roll and Scattered Noise Attenuation
authors: Dawei Liu, Mauricio D. Sacchi, Xiang Wang, Wenchao Chen
abstract: This paper proposes an unsupervised deep learning approach to attenuate two types of coherent noise in land seismic data: ground roll (surface waves) and near-surface scattered energy. The method eliminates the need for labeled training data by leveraging the inherent low-frequency bias of generator networks, which naturally learn self-similar features during training. The approach combines Linear Moveout (LMO) correction, Normal Moveout (NMO) correction, and generator network to extract desired components based on self-similarity.

title: A Dual Attention Denoising Network for DAS VSP Signal Recovery and Its Interpretability Analysis
authors: Man Zhang, Yue Li, Yuxing Zhao, Yibo Wang
abstract: The paper proposes a Dual Attention Denoising Network (DADN) that combines Spatial Attention (SA) and Channel Attention (CA) integrated into Double Attention Blocks (DAB) within an encoder-decoder architecture for DAS VSP data denoising. The network aims to improve recovery of deep-layer weak signals. The paper also uses Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) for interpretability analysis to visualize what features the network learns.

title: A Universal Denoiser for DAS-VSP Data Based on Semantic Mask and Random Mask Training
authors: Fuyao Sun, Yue Li
abstract: The paper proposes a universal deep learning denoiser called Triplet Masking Denoiser (Tri-MD) for distributed acoustic sensing-vertical seismic profiling (DAS-VSP) data. The method uses random masking during training to force the model to perform signal recovery rather than simple noise separation, semantic masking using a segmentation subnetwork to estimate signal location and create semantic masks of seismic effective signals, and attention mask during inference to ensure test consistency with training. This approach allows the denoiser to generalize well even when trained on datasets with limited noise variety.

title: Analysis of DAS Seismic Noise Generation and Elimination Process Based on Mean-SDE Diffusion Model
authors: Qiankun Feng, Shigang Wang, Yue Li
abstract: This paper proposes a Mean-Stochastic Differential Equation (Mean-SDE) diffusion model for DAS seismic noise generation and elimination. Key contributions include: Modified forward diffusion process transforms the final state into a combination of noisy DAS VSP data and Gaussian noise, enabling precise representation of multi-type noise generation; Enhanced noise prediction uses a Nonlinear Activation Free Network (NAFnet) with a time Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) for accurate noise predictions at different states; Improved training stability modifies the objective function to seek the optimal trajectory for best signal reconstruction quality.

title: Efficient Seismic Data Denoising via Deep Learning With Improved MCA-SCUNet
authors: J. Chen, G. Chen, J. Li, R. Du, Y. Qi, C. Li, N. Wang
abstract: In hydrocarbon exploration, seismic data collected in the field inevitably encounter noise interference, which subsequently affects the data processing and interpretation. Recently, deep learning methods have gained widespread popularity in seismic denoising. Among these methods, the U-Net has shown some potential, but its performance in complex noise suppression needs further improvement due to the limitations of the U-Net structure. This article proposes a novel noise suppression method based on an encoder-decoder architecture called multiscale channel attention Swin Conv U-Net (MCA-SCUNet). It enhances the U-Net through the integration of Swin-Conv block (SCB) and Multiscale channel attention block (MCAB).

title: Fast Dictionary Learning Based on Data-Driven Tight Frame for 3-D Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Z. Zhou, J. Wu, M. Bai, B. Yang, Z. Ma
abstract: The paper proposes a novel data-driven tight frame (DDTF) dictionary learning method with an overcomplete dictionary constructed by discrete cosine transform (DCT) for 3-D seismic data denoising. The main contributions include computational efficiency: only one singular value decomposition (SVD) is required to update the entire dictionary, significantly accelerating the computational efficiency of 3-D seismic data denoising. The method significantly reduces computational burden compared to state-of-the-art methods while achieving better denoising performance than traditional DDTF methods.

title: Gabor-Based Learnable Sparse Representation for Self-Supervised Denoising
authors: Sixiu Liu, Shijun Cheng, Tariq Alkhalifah
abstract: The paper proposes a physics-informed deep unfolding approach for self-supervised denoising that uses Gabor filters with learnable parameters. The method is designed to suppress different noise types (including pseudo-random noise and ground roll in seismic data) without requiring clean training labels, making it applicable to real-world scenarios where clean data is unavailable.

title: Interpretable Unsupervised Learning Framework for Multidimensional Erratic and Random Noise Attenuation
authors: Liuqing Yang, Sergey Fomel, Shoudong Wang, Xiaohong Chen, Yaoguang Sun, Yangkang Chen
abstract: The paper proposes an unsupervised learning-based Multibranch Attention Network (MANet) for attenuating both erratic and random noise in 2D and 3D seismic data. Key features include: No need for manually generated labels (unsupervised approach); Robust mean-Huber loss function for handling erratic noise (non-Gaussian, high-amplitude outliers); Skip connections for enhanced feature migration from shallow to deep levels; Visualization of weight matrices and encoders for improved interpretability.

title: Joint-Guided Denoising Network for Erratic Noise Attenuation
authors: Tie Zhong, Ming Cheng, Sibo Wang, Shiqi Dong, Shaoping Lu, Xintong Dong
abstract: The paper proposes a Joint-Guided Denoising Network (JGD-Net) for attenuating erratic noise in seismic exploration data. Unlike conventional CNN-based methods that struggle with intense erratic noise due to limited feature-interaction capabilities, JGD-Net employs a joint-guided scheme and an attention mechanism. The network is designed to handle non-Gaussian, large-amplitude erratic noise that violates the assumptions of traditional least-squares based denoising methods. The authors also designed a novel loss function based on L1 norm and hyperbolic tangent function to improve training optimization.

title: Seismic Data Denoising Using a New Framework of FABEMD-Based Dictionary Learning
authors: Weiqi Wang, Jidong Yang, Zhenchun Li, Jianping Huang, Chong Zhao
abstract: The paper proposes a noise suppression method that combines Dictionary Learning (DL) with Fast Adaptive Bidirectional Empirical Mode Decomposition (FABEMD). The method leverages FABEMD's advantages in multi-scale signal decomposition along with DL's efficient sparse representation capabilities to achieve noise suppression for low signal-to-noise ratio (LSNR) seismic signals. The authors group bi-dimensional intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) based on cross-correlation coefficients and train dictionaries using the sequential generalization K-means (SGK) method, enhancing computational efficiency and adaptability compared to traditional K-SVD approaches.

title: Seismic Data Strong Noise Attenuation Based on Diffusion Model and Principal Component Analysis
authors: Junheng Peng, Yong Li, Zhangquan Liao, Xuben Wang, Xingyu Yang
abstract: The paper proposes using diffusion model theory combined with Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for seismic data noise attenuation. The main innovations include: Using the Bayesian equation to reverse the noise addition process in a step-by-step manner; Employing PCA to estimate the noise level in blind Gaussian seismic data, which determines the number of steps needed for the reverse denoising process; Training on synthetic data and validating on field data through transfer learning. The method is particularly effective for strong noise scenarios where conventional methods often fail.

title: Self-Supervised Pretraining Transformer for Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Hongzhou Wang, Jun Lin, Yue Li, Xintong Dong, Xunqian Tong, Shaoping Lu
abstract: The paper proposes a Transformer-based model called Seismic Data Denoising Transformer (SDT) that uses self-attention mechanisms to overcome CNN limitations in capturing long-range dependencies and global coherence. It introduces a novel self-supervised pretraining strategy using a large-scale dataset and effectively handles complex seismic noise attenuation while preserving weak signal amplitudes.

title: Self-Supervised Seismic Swell Noise Suppression From Noisy Seismic Data
authors: Weiwei Xu, Vincenzo Lipari, Paolo Bestagini, Wenchao Chen, Stefano Tubaro
abstract: This paper applies self-supervised learning techniques specifically for swell noise suppression in seismic data, a type of coherent noise that is particularly challenging to remove from marine seismic recordings. The work aims to reduce the dependency on large amounts of labeled (clean-noisy paired) training data that is often difficult or impossible to obtain in geophysical applications.

title: SelfTexture: Self-Supervised Learning for Spatially Correlated Noise Removal of DAS VSP Data via Adaptive Texture Analysis
authors: Shiqi Zhu, Haixia Zhao, Tingting Bai, Wenchao Chen, Yuanzhong Chen
abstract: The paper proposes SelfTexture, a self-supervised learning method for removing spatially correlated noise from DAS VSP data. Key innovations include: Neighborhood-Mask Network (NMN) that carefully designs the receptive field to handle correlated noise; Texture Total Variation (TTV) Regularization to eliminate correlated noise while preserving local smooth structure of effective signals; Asymmetric-Dilated Convolution (ADConv) with large receptive field in specific directions to maximize texture difference between signal and noise.

title: Semi-Supervised DAS VSP Data Denoising Using Signal and Noise Distribution Difference
authors: Man Zhang, Juan Li, Yuxing Zhao, Ning Wu, Yue Li
abstract: This paper proposes a semi-supervised deep learning denoising method for DAS VSP data. The key innovation is exploiting the distribution difference between signal and noise to enable effective denoising with reduced reliance on labeled training data. The method combines a supervised path trained on synthetic data to extract rich signal features, and an unsupervised path that leverages the distribution characteristics of field data to extract realistic signal features.

title: Stochastic Solutions for Simultaneous Seismic Data Denoising and Reconstruction via Score-Based Generative Models
authors: Chuangji Meng, Jinghuai Gao, Yajun Tian, Hongling Chen, Wei Zhang, Renyu Luo
abstract: The paper proposes stochastic solutions for simultaneous seismic data denoising and reconstruction via score-based generative models. The method leverages score-based generative models to provide probabilistic solutions for seismic data recovery, enabling simultaneous handling of both noise attenuation and missing data reconstruction.

title: STUGAN: An Integrated Swin Transformer-Based Generative Adversarial Networks for Seismic Data Reconstruction and Denoising
authors: Yan Zhang, Yiming Zhang, Hongli Dong, Liwei Song
abstract: The paper introduces STUGAN, a novel deep learning model that combines Swin Transformer (ST) with Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) for simultaneous seismic data reconstruction and denoising. The ST module replaces traditional convolution modules in GAN, using self-attention mechanisms to capture both local and non-local seismic data features. The generator is based on a U-Net architecture. Conditional constraints are incorporated into the discriminator to guide gradient optimization.

title: Unsupervised Intense VSP Coupling Noise Suppression With Iterative Robust Deep Learning
authors: Feng Qian, Haowei Hua, Yuhang Wen, Jingjing Zong, Gulan Zhang, Guangmin Hu
abstract: The paper proposes an Iterative Robust Deep Convolutional Autoencoder (IRDCAE) model that suppresses intense VSP coupling noise caused by poorly coupled geophones in boreholes. It uses weighted column sparsity (WCS) to characterize coupling noise behavior, operates in an unsupervised manner without requiring assumptions about valuable signals, and combines advantages of both Robust Principal Component Analysis (RPCA) and Deep Convolutional Autoencoder (DCAE).

title: Unsupervised Learning With Waveform Multibranch Attention Mechanism for Erratic Noise Attenuation
authors: Chao Fu, Yupeng Huo, Guodong Li, Yangkang Chen
abstract: The paper proposes an unsupervised learning-based multibranch attention network (MANet) to attenuate erratic and random noise in 2D and 3D seismic data. Key features include: Waveform multibranch attention mechanism for extracting global features; Skip connections between encoder and decoder for enhanced feature migration; Robust mean-Huber loss function less sensitive to outliers; Improved interpretability through visualization of weight matrices and encoders.

title: Wavelet-GAN: A GPR Noise and Clutter Removal Method Based on Small Real Datasets
authors: Ge Junkai, Sun Huaifeng, Shao Wei, Liu Dong, Liu Hao, Zhao Feng, Tian Bo, Liu Shucai
abstract: This paper proposes a Wavelet-GAN network that combines Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) with Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to remove noise and clutter from Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) images. The method is particularly notable for its ability to perform well when trained on small real datasets, which is a significant advantage since obtaining large amounts of labeled GPR data is often challenging.

title: A Self-Supervised Method for Attenuating Seismic Random and Tracewise Coherent Noise Under the Nonpixelwise Independence Assumption
authors: Chuangji Meng, Jinghuai Gao, Wenting Shang, Yajun Tian
abstract: The paper addresses limitations of existing self-supervised deep learning methods for seismic noise attenuation. Common self-supervised methods rely on the pixelwise independence assumption, which does not match real field seismic noise characteristics. The proposed method uses a generalized blind neighborhood concept where the blind neighborhood size controls how much spatial correlation is disrupted, allowing handling of random noise with varying spatial correlation.

title: DCAF-Net: An Intelligent Denoising Method for Microseismic Signals Based on the Combination of Time and Wavelet Domains
authors: Guanqun Sheng, Jinyu Pang, Wei Chen, Xingguang Tang
abstract: DCAF-Net (Dual Cross-Attention Fusion Network) is designed for microseismic signal denoising by combining time domain information and wavelet domain information (using Discrete Wavelet Transform - DWT). Main technical contributions include: Replaces pooling layers with Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) for downsampling to capture frequency information; Introduces a Spatial-Transformer Attention Block (STAB) based on Transformer encoding structure for improved feature extraction; Proposes an Adaptive Attention Fusion Block (AAFB) for enhanced multi-scale data fusion.

title: Efficient Unsupervised Deep Learning for Simultaneous Seismic Noise Attenuation and Interpolation
authors: Anyu Li, Wei Chen, X. Wei, H. Zhang, et al.
abstract: This paper presents an unsupervised deep learning approach for the efficient reconstruction of noisy and incomplete seismic data. The main contributions include: Reconstruction Error-Based Patch Selection (REBPS); Encoder-decoder network with POCS (Projection Onto Convex Sets); 2D/3D data divided into 1D signals via patching; REBPS selects patches with more complex structures for network input. The method was validated on both synthetic and field seismic data.

title: Five-Dimensional Intelligent Denoising via Transformer
authors: Yang Xiang, Jianwei Ma, Wei Wang
abstract: The paper addresses 5D seismic exploration data denoising using a Transformer-based approach. The 5D data consists of two spatial coordinates for shot points, two spatial coordinates for receiver points, and one temporal coordinate. The authors introduce a Transformer model with 5-D positional embedding to exploit structural information in higher-dimensional space for effective seismic denoising. The framework also supports high-dimensional seismic data interpolation and reconstruction through a pretraining-fine-tuning procedure.

title: Zero-Shot Denoising for DAS-VSP Data Based on Conditional Diffusion Probabilistic Models
authors: Dawei Liu, Zhenyu Wang, Xiaokai Wang, Wenchao Chen
abstract: The paper introduces a conditional denoising diffusion probabilistic model (C-DDPM) for zero-shot denoising of DAS VSP data. Technical contributions include: Adaptive FK conditioning approach incorporated into the diffusion process; Asymmetric dilated convolution (ADConv) for effective noise suppression; Strong a priori extraction capabilities for handling different noise combinations. Tested on both synthetic and real-world seismic datasets, demonstrating improved noise reduction and signal clarity.

title: ZSN2N-DAS: Zero-Shot Noise2Noise Denoising for Distributed Acoustic Sensing Data
authors: Dingrong Feng, Zhiguo Wang, Jinghuai Gao
abstract: ZSN2N-DAS is a self-supervised learning method that achieves zero-shot Noise2Noise (N2N) denoising for ground-based DAS data without requiring training data. Key innovation mathematically proves equivalence leveraging the fact that noise in raw DAS data is unstructured and has zero mean. Compared to the leading deep learning model N2N-DAS, ZSN2N-DAS achieves 54% reduction in network parameters, ~20% improvement in SNR of seismic signals, and ~28% increase in average number of effectively picked arrivals. Validated on datasets from a DAS array on the Rutford Ice Stream in Antarctica.

title: Attention-Based Neural Network for Erratic Noise Attenuation From Seismic Data With a Shuffled Noise Training Data Generation Strategy
authors: Shaowen Wang, Peng Song, Jun Tan, Bingshou He, Qianqian Wang, Guoning Du
abstract: The paper proposes an attention-based neural network specifically designed for erratic noise attenuation in seismic data. The network outputs both an attention map (showing spatial distribution probability of erratic noise) and raw noise. A novel shuffled noise training data generation strategy is introduced for creating training datasets from conventionally denoised data. The method allows manual control of denoising capability by filtering the attention map with probability, preserving reflections in noise-free areas while effectively removing erratic noise.

title: Automated Event Detection and Denoising Method for Passive Seismic Data Using Residual Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: A. Othman, N. Iqbal, S. M. Hanafy, U. B. Waheed
abstract: The paper proposes an automated event detection and denoising method for passive seismic data using residual deep convolutional neural networks. The method combines event detection and denoising in a single framework, leveraging residual connections in deep CNNs to effectively suppress noise while preserving seismic event signals in passive seismic recordings.

title: Deep Learning for Simultaneous Seismic Image Super-Resolution and Denoising
authors: Jintao Li, Xinming Wu, Zhanxuan Hu
abstract: The paper addresses enhancing seismic images by simultaneously performing super-resolution and denoising using deep learning. The method handles both tasks jointly, which are typically handled separately in traditional seismic data processing workflows.

title: A Method for Denoising Seismic Signals With a CNN Based on an Attention Mechanism
authors: Zhang Min, Xu Yi
abstract: This paper introduces an STCNN (Sparse and Dense Attention CNN) network with a retractable attention mechanism that has both dense and sparse self-attention modules. It alternates between dense self-attention (D-MSA) and sparse self-attention (S-MSA), combining CNN's local modeling with Transformer's global modeling capabilities. It outperforms U-Net and DnCNN in suppressing noise while preserving effective seismic information, significantly improving signal-to-noise ratio on both synthetic and field data.

title: BSnet An Unsupervised Blind Spot Network for Seismic Data Random Noise Attenuation
