authors: Wenqian Fang, Lihua Fu, Hongwei Li, Shaoyong Liu, Qin Wang
abstract: The paper proposes BSnet, an unsupervised deep learning method for seismic data random noise attenuation using the blind spot strategy. It uses a modified U-Net architecture with two types of random mask operators for Gaussian white noise and bandpass noise, enabling training directly on noisy data without clean labels.

title: Deblending of Seismic Data Based on Neural Network Trained in the CSG
authors: Kunxi Wang, Tianyue Hu
abstract: The paper presents an iterative deblending method using a deep neural network trained in the Common Shot Gather (CSG) domain. It uses double-blended simultaneous source data as input and blended CSG data as labels, solving the problem of difficult acquisition of clean label data and making the network applicable to different geological areas without retraining.

title: Deblending of Simultaneous-Source Seismic Data Based on Deep Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Jingwang Cheng, Chuncheng Liu, Li Zhou, Wei Chen, Hanming Gu
abstract: The paper proposes a data-driven deep learning method for fast seismic deblending using convolutional neural networks. It sorts blended data from common source to common channel domain to transform blending noise from coherent to incoherent, trained on field seismic data with over 20,000 examples.

title: Denoising Deep Learning Network Based on Singular Spectrum Analysis—DAS Seismic Data Denoising With Multichannel SVDDCNN
authors: Qiankun Feng, Yue Li
abstract: The paper combines Singular Spectrum Analysis with deep learning for DAS seismic data denoising using a multichannel Singular Value Decomposition Denoising Convolutional Neural Network (SVDDCNN).

title: Desert Seismic Low-Frequency Noise Attenuation Using Low-Rank Decomposition-Based Denoising Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Haitao Ma, Yue Wang, Yue Li, Yuxing Zhao
abstract: The paper proposes ADMM-CNN that combines low-rank decomposition with feed-forward DnCNN for desert seismic data denoising. It decomposes data into low-rank, sparse, and perturbation layers as inputs to a three-channel neural network, specifically addressing desert seismic data challenges including nonstationarity and low-frequency noise characteristics.

title: Distributed Acoustic Sensing Vertical Seismic Profile Data Denoiser Based on Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Yuxing Zhao, Yue Li, Ning Wu
abstract: The paper develops a Convolutional Neural Network denoiser tailored for DAS-VSP data using hybrid training sets combining synthetic and field data, addressing coupled noise reduction in distributed acoustic sensing seismic data.

title: Distributed Acoustic Sensing Vertical Seismic Profile Data Denoising Based on Multistage Denoising Network
authors: Yue Li, Ming Zhang, Yuxing Zhao, Ning Wu
abstract: The paper proposes a multistage denoising network for DAS-VSP data, progressively denoising through multiple stages to better preserve seismic signals while removing noise.

title: Erratic Noise Attenuation Using Double Sparsity Dictionary Learning Method
authors: Dokku Tejaswi, Sunil Chinnadurai, Karthikeyan Elumalai
abstract: The paper proposes Double Sparsity Dictionary Learning combining analytical transforms and adaptive dictionary learning for seismic data denoising. It addresses both random noise and erratic noise using sequential analytical and adaptive transformations.

title: Ground Truth-Free 3-D Seismic Random Noise Attenuation via Deep Tensor Convolutional Neural Networks in the Time-Frequency Domain
authors: Feng Qian, Zhangbo Liu, Yan Wang, Yingjie Zhou, Guangmin Hu
abstract: The paper proposes SURE-TCNN, a ground truth-free approach using deep tensor convolutional neural networks in the time-frequency domain for 3-D seismic random noise attenuation. It uses Stein's Unbiased Risk Estimate as the loss function instead of supervised MSE.

title: Learning From Noisy Data An Unsupervised Random Denoising Method for Seismic Data Using Model-Based Deep Learning
authors: Feng Wang, Bo Yang, Yuqing Wang, Ming Wang
abstract: The paper proposes an unsupervised denoising method based on model-based deep learning combining domain knowledge with data-driven approaches. It uses modified ISTA with trainable dictionary and code parameters, and f-x deconvolution output as input, progressively improving without needing a separate testing phase.

title: LOUD Local Orthogonalization-Constrained Unsupervised Deep-Learning Denoiser
authors: Zhicheng Geng, Yangkang Chen, Sergey Fomel, Luming Liang
abstract: LOUD is an unsupervised deep learning method using a deep convolutional autoencoder with local signal-and-noise orthogonalization as a constraint in the loss function for seismic random noise attenuation. The orthogonality constraint ensures removed noise is orthogonal to recovered signal.

title: Low-Frequency Seismic Noise Reduction Based on Deep Complex ReactionDiffusion Model
authors: Yushu Zhang, Hongbo Lin, Yue Li, Haitao Ma, Guijin Yao
abstract: The paper proposes a deep complex reaction-diffusion model specifically designed for reducing low-frequency seismic noise. The method combines complex-valued neural networks to handle the complex nature of seismic data in the frequency domain with reaction-diffusion processes for iterative noise reduction.

title: Multi-Scale Progressive Fusion Attention Network Based on Small Sample Training for DAS Noise Suppression
authors: Ning Wu, Tong Xing, Yue Li
abstract: The paper proposes a Multi-scale Progressive Fusion Attention Network (MPFAN) with pyramidal structure trained using small samples for DAS noise suppression. It uses a multi-scale approach with recurrent calculation and attention mechanism to explore correlations of complex DAS noise and learn global texture.

title: Multidimensional Seismic Data Denoising Using Framelet-Based Order-p Tensor Deep Learning
authors: Feng Qian, Yan Wang, Bingwei Zheng, Zhangbo Liu, Yingjie Zhou, Guangmin Hu
abstract: The paper introduces FPTNN (Framelet-based Order-p Tensor Neural Network) for multidimensional seismic data denoising. It redefines order-p t-product using framelet transform, directly processing 4D prestack and 5D wide-azimuth seismic data without flattening, preserving global structure.

title: Multigranularity Feature Fusion Convolutional Neural Network for Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Jun Feng, Chaoxian Chen, Xingming Liu, Changqing Chen
abstract: The paper proposes MFFCNN using multiple convolution kernel sizes in parallel for multigranularity feature extraction and fusion for seismic random noise removal. It overcomes the limitation of traditional CNNs using fixed-size kernels by extracting features from different granularities simultaneously.

title: Multiscale Residual Pyramid Network for Seismic Background Noise Attenuation
authors: Tie Zhong, Feng Li, Rongzhe Zhang, Xintong Dong, Shaoping Lu
abstract: The paper proposes MRP-Net (Multiscale Residual Pyramid Network) with double-path mechanism for extracting multi-scale features with residual connections for complex seismic background noise attenuation.

title: Multiscale Spatial Attention Network for Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Xintong Dong, Jun Lin, Shaoping Lu, Hongzhou Wang, Yue Li
abstract: The paper proposes a multiscale spatial attention network for seismic data denoising, combining deep learning techniques with attention mechanisms to effectively remove noise from seismic data while preserving signal characteristics.

title: NS2NS Self-Learning for Seismic Image Denoising
authors: Naihao Liu, Jiale Wang, Jinghuai Gao, Kai Yu, Yihuai Lou, Yitao Pu, Shaojie Chang
abstract: NS2NS is an unsupervised self-learning method for seismic image denoising using only single noisy seismic data without clean labels. It leverages self-similarity of seismic data and spatial independence of incoherent noise with a Bernoulli sampler for fusion.

title: Seismic Intelligent Deblending via Plug and Play Method With Blended CSGs Trained Deep CNN Gaussian Denoiser
authors: Weiwei Xu, Yanhui Zhou, Dawei Liu, Xiang Wang, Wenchao Chen
abstract: The paper presents a plug and play method for seismic deblending using a CNN Gaussian denoiser trained on blended Common Shot Gathers (CSGs), eliminating the need for clean label data. The trained denoiser is integrated into the ADMM algorithm, achieving convergence to a fixed point.

title: Seismic Random Noise Attenuation Based on Non-IID Pixel-Wise Gaussian Noise Modeling
authors: Chuangji Meng, Jinghuai Gao, Yajun Tian, Zhiqiang Wang
abstract: The paper proposes VI-Non-IID, modeling noise as non-IID pixel-wise Gaussian where each pixel can have different noise variance. It integrates noise attenuation and noise level estimation into a unified Bayesian framework using variational inference, enabling explicit prediction of both denoised data and pixel-wise noise level maps.

title: Seismic Random Noise Attenuation by Applying Multiscale Denoising Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Tie Zhong, Ming Cheng, Xintong Dong, Ning Wu
abstract: The paper uses a multiscale denoising convolutional neural network for seismic random noise attenuation. The multiscale approach captures noise characteristics at different scales, which is particularly important for seismic data where noise can have complex, scale-dependent properties.

title: Seismic Signal Matching and Complex Noise Suppression by Zernike Moments and Trilateral Weighted Sparse Coding
authors: Chao Zhang, Mirko van der Baan
abstract: The paper addresses complex noise suppression in seismic data through Zernike moments for rotation-invariant signal matching and trilateral weighted sparse coding with three weight matrices for characterizing complex noise properties and signal sparsity priors.

title: Self-Attention Deep Image Prior Network for Unsupervised 3-D Seismic Data Enhancement
authors: Omar M. Saad, Yapo Abolé Serge Innocent Oboué, Min Bai, Lotfy Samy, Liuqing Yang, Yangkang Chen
abstract: The paper introduces ADNet combining self-attention mechanisms with deep image prior for unsupervised 3D seismic data enhancement including denoising, interpolation, and reconstruction. The Attention-based DenseNet Architecture uses self-attention to capture long-range dependencies.

title: Self-Supervised Multitask 3-D Partial Convolutional Neural Network for Random Noise Attenuation and Reconstruction in 3-D Seismic Data
authors: Wei Cao, Ying Shi, Weihong Wang, Xuebao Guo, Feng Tian, Yang Zhao
abstract: The paper presents a self-supervised multitask 3-D partial convolutional neural network for simultaneous random noise attenuation and missing trace reconstruction in 3-D seismic data. The partial convolution mechanism is effective for handling irregularly missing data.

title: Similarity-Informed Self-Learning and Its Application on Seismic Image Denoising
authors: Naihao Liu, Jiale Wang, Jinghuai Gao, Shaojie Chang, Y. Lou
abstract: The paper proposes Similarity-Informed Self-Learning (SISL) using a similar image sampler leveraging the self-similarity of noisy seismic images to construct training pairs without needing clean target data. A hybrid loss function with regularization constraint effectively attenuates random noise while preserving valid seismic events.

title: StorSeismic A New Paradigm in Deep Learning for Seismic Processing
authors: Randy Harsuko, Tariq A. Alkhalifah
abstract: StorSeismic introduces a BERT-based paradigm for seismic processing with pre-training and fine-tuning framework. It captures and stores key seismic features from data and adapts to downstream tasks with minimal parameter changes, handling denoising, interpolation, first-break picking, and velocity model prediction.

title: Study of Parameters in Dictionary Learning Method for Seismic Denoising
authors: Lakshmi Kuruguntla, Vineela Chandra Dodda, Karthikeyan Elumalai
abstract: The paper investigates how various parameters in dictionary learning affect seismic data denoising performance, including patch size, dictionary size, number of training patches, choice of threshold, sparsity level, and computational cost. Evaluations use SNR and MSE on synthetic and field seismic data.

title: Unsupervised 3-D Random Noise Attenuation Using Deep Skip Autoencoder
authors: Liuqing Yang, Shoudong Wang, Xiaohong Chen, Omar M. Saad, Wei Chen, Yapo Abolé Serge Innocent Oboué, Yangkang Chen
abstract: The paper proposes DDUL (deep-denoising unsupervised learning) using a fully symmetrical deep skip autoencoder for attenuating random noise in 2-D and 3-D seismic data without requiring clean labels for training.

title: Unsupervised Deep Learning for Single-Channel Earthquake Data Denoising and Its Applications in Event Detection and Fully Automatic Location
authors: O. M. Saad, Y. F. Chen, A. Savvaidis, W. Chen, F. Zhang, Y. Chen
abstract: The paper proposes an unsupervised deep learning approach for single-channel earthquake data denoising using attention networks in the time-frequency domain with STFT. It creates a binary mask through adaptive thresholding and achieves improved SNR over supervised methods without needing labeled training data.

title: Unsupervised Erratic Seismic Noise Attenuation With Robust Deep Convolutional Autoencoders
authors: Feng Qian, Wei Guo, Zhangbo Liu, Hongtao Yu, Gulan Zhang, Guangmin Hu
abstract: The paper presents RDCAE (Robust Deep Convolutional Autoencoder) for erratic-plus-Gaussian noise removal. It replaces MSE loss with the smooth Welsch function for robust denoising, downweighting erratic noise using a curbed weight and diluting random noise by combining mean square with total variation.

title: Attribute-Based Double Constraint Denoising Network for Seismic Data
authors: Shengnan Wang, Yue Li, Ning Wu, Yuxing Zhao, Haiyang Yao
abstract: Att-DCDN uses encoder-decoder architecture with an attribute classifier to form a GAN, attenuating seismic noise by controlling with/without target attributes. It uses double-constraint training with reconstruction/residual losses for signal recovery and attribute classification/adversarial losses for noise attenuation.

title: Automated Platform for Microseismic Signal Analysis: Denoising, Detection, and Classification in Slope Stability Studies
authors: Jiangfeng Li, Lina Stankovic, Stella Pytharouli, Vladimir Stankovic
abstract: The paper presents an automated end-to-end system for microseismic signal analysis focused on slope stability studies. The platform integrates denoising, detection, and classification of microseismic events, designed to process big datasets of continuous seismic recordings quickly as an early warning system for landslide prediction.

title: Deep Learning Seismic Random Noise Attenuation via Improved Residual Convolutional Neural Network
authors: L. Yang, W. Chen, H. Wang, Y. Chen
abstract: The paper proposes an adaptive random noise attenuation framework based on CNNs with residual learning, transforming the target from signal learning to noise learning. It uses batch normalization, leaky activation functions, and double convolutional residual blocks to improve training efficiency and generalization.

title: Intelligent Deblending of Seismic Data Based on U-Net and Transfer Learning
authors: Benfeng Wang, Jiakuo Li, Jingrui Luo, Yingying Wang, Jianhua Geng
abstract: The paper applies U-Net architecture with transfer learning for seismic data deblending. The network is trained on synthetic data and fine-tuned for field data, effectively separating blended seismic sources.

title: Incoherent Noise Suppression of Seismic Data Based on Robust Low-Rank Approximation
authors: Mi Zhang, Yang Liu, Haoran Ren, Yangkang Chen
abstract: The paper proposes suppressing seismic incoherent noise based on robust low-rank approximation. It transforms seismic data into Hankel matrices and uses a mixed nuclear norm and l1-norm model with orthonormal subspace learning, achieving high computational efficiency without SVD.

title: Low-Frequency Desert Noise Intelligent Suppression in Seismic Data Based on Multiscale Geometric Analysis Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Yuxing Zhao, Yue Li, Baojun Yang
abstract: The paper proposes NC-CNN using non-subsampled contourlet transform (NSCT) as multiscale geometric analysis for intelligent denoising of desert seismic data. It addresses low-frequency noise sharing the same frequency band with effective signals, achieving good results even under low SNR conditions.

title: New Suppression Technology for Low-Frequency Noise in Desert Region: The Improved Robust Principal Component Analysis Based on Prediction of Neural Network
authors: Xintong Dong, Tie Zhong, Yue Li
abstract: The paper proposes RPCA combined with CNN prediction for desert low-frequency noise suppression. It extracts effective signals from both low-rank and sparse matrix components using CNN models trained on synthetic seismic events with real desert noise, significantly improving SNR.

title: Poststack Seismic Data Denoising Based on 3-D Convolutional Neural Network
authors: D. Liu, W. Wang, X. Wang, C. Wang, J. Pei, W. Chen
abstract: The paper proposes a 3-D convolutional neural network for poststack seismic data denoising, introducing one of the first 3D CNN architectures specifically designed for this task.

title: Automatic Source Localization and Attenuation of Seismic Interference Noise Using Density-Based Clustering Method
authors: Pengcheng Xu, Wenkai Lu, Benfeng Wang
abstract: The paper uses DBSCAN clustering to localize external sources causing seismic interference noise in marine seismic data, then attenuates the noise based on detected source locations using singular value decomposition.

title: Seismic Noise Attenuation Using Unsupervised Sparse Feature Learning
authors: Mi Zhang, Yang Liu, Min Bai, Yangkang Chen
abstract: The paper presents an unsupervised sparse feature learning approach using dictionary learning and sparse representation for seismic noise attenuation without requiring labeled training data.

title: Seismic Signal Denoising and Decomposition Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Weiqiang Zhu, S. Mostafa Mousavi, Gregory C. Beroza
abstract: The paper develops DeepDenoiser, learning simultaneous sparse representation in time-frequency domain and non-linear mapping to masks, decomposing input data into signal of interest and noise.

title: Structured Graph Dictionary Learning and Application on the Seismic Denoising
authors: Lina Liu, Jianwei Ma
abstract: The paper proposes Structured Graph Dictionary Learning incorporating local and nonlocal similarities into dictionary learning through a structured graph, using smooth atoms and SSC-GSM model for seismic denoising.

id: Renze-一种基于runet卷积神经网络-2020
title: 一种基于RUnet卷积神经网络的地震资料随机噪声压制方法
authors: Luo Renze, Li Yangyang
abstract: The paper proposes RUnet combining residual learning with U-Net structure for random noise attenuation in seismic data.

id: Cuiqian-基于全局上下文和注意力机制深度-2021
title: 基于全局上下文和注意力机制深度卷积神经网络的地震数据去噪
authors: Yang Cuiqian, Zhou Yatong, He Hao, Cui Tao, Wang Yang
abstract: The paper proposes a deep convolutional neural network combining global context and attention mechanism with residual learning for seismic random noise suppression, improving preservation of micro-scale features.

title: 基于CWT-CNN的地震噪声压制研究
authors: Zhao Jinquan, You Jiachun, Wei Junting, Huang Cong
abstract: The paper combines Continuous Wavelet Transform with CNN for seismic noise suppression, leveraging wavelet transform multi-resolution analysis and deep learning feature extraction capabilities.

title: 基于U-Net网络的二维小波域地震数据随机噪声衰减
authors: Qiu Yi, Bao Qianzong, Ma Ming, Liu Zhishui
abstract: The paper proposes Dwt-U-Net combining 2D discrete wavelet transform with U-Net, training on wavelet coefficients to attenuate random noise with approximately half the training time of traditional U-Net while improving SNR.

id: Lei-基于密集扩张卷积残差网络的地震-2023
title: 基于密集扩张卷积残差网络的地震数据随机噪声压制方法
authors: Gao Lei, Shen Housen, Min Fan
abstract: The paper proposes DDCRN using dense blocks, multi-scale dilated convolution, feature fusion, and residual structures for seismic random noise suppression, outperforming K-SVD, f-x deconvolution, DnCNN, and U-Net.

title: 基于Swin-Transformer与生成对抗网络的地震随机噪声压制方法
authors: Zhou Hongshuai, Cheng Bingjie, Xu Tianji
abstract: The paper proposes ST-GAN using Swin-Transformer as generator and CNN as discriminator, combining L1 loss and adversarial loss for seismic random noise suppression, avoiding over-smoothing and false reflections.

id: Chen-基于多尺度卷积自编码器的地震噪-2024
title: 基于多尺度卷积自编码器的地震噪声智能压制方法及应用
authors: Xie Chen, Xu Tianji, Qian Zhongping, Shen Jie, Liu Sheng, Tang Jianming, Wen Xuekang
abstract: The paper proposes a multi-scale convolutional auto-encoder with InceptionV4 modules and spatial soft attention, using transfer learning from synthetic to field data for intelligent suppression of both random and coherent noise.

id: Yankai-生成式对抗神经网络的改进及其在-2024
title: 生成式对抗神经网络的改进及其在地震数据压噪中的应用
authors: PENG Hailong,  LI Ming,  SUN Wenzhao,  LI Lie,  ZHOU Fan,  LU Tongxiang,  JIANG Fan
abstract: The paper improves GAN for seismic data random noise suppression, combining U-Net structure and attention mechanisms to address limitations of traditional methods in handling complex noise.

title: 基于MSIP GAN的地震数据随机噪声压制方法
authors: Luo Renze, Li Zhiqi
abstract: The paper proposes MSIP GAN (Multi-Scale Information Perception GAN) with parallel multi-scale modules, multi-channel information fusion, and consistency regularization for seismic random noise suppression.

id: Donghong-基于无监督学习网络的三维地震随-2025
title: 基于无监督学习网络的三维地震随机噪声衰减方法研究
authors: Zhou Donghong
abstract: The paper proposes an end-to-end unsupervised learning framework for 3D seismic random noise attenuation using DLNet with skip connections, splitting multidimensional data into small 1D patches for iterative training without clean labels.

id: Lei-优化损失函数的低信噪比微地震信-2023
title: 优化损失函数的低信噪比微地震信号去噪方法
authors: GAO Hongyu, ,  SONG Xueyan,  ZHANG Yiwen,  HAO Fenghua
abstract: The paper optimizes the loss function for low SNR microseismic signal denoising, improving the network ability to recover weak signals in microseismic data with poor signal-to-noise ratio.

id: Guangde-基于cnn模型的地震数据噪声压-2025
title: 基于CNN模型的地震数据噪声压制性能对比研究
authors: Zhang Guangde, Zhang Huaibang, Zhao Jinquan, You Jiachun, Wei Junting, Yang Dekuan
abstract: The paper compares five CNN-based models (FCN, U-Net, CBDNet, SwinUnet, TransUnet) with continuous wavelet transform preprocessing for seismic data denoising, evaluating performance on random, swell, and ground-roll noise.

id: Zhencong-基于结构保护去噪神经网络的地震-2024
title: 基于结构保护去噪神经网络的地震数据随机噪声压制
authors: Zhao Zhencong, Rao Ying
abstract: The paper proposes a structure-preserving denoising CNN embedding local dip information as physical constraints to avoid over-smoothing complex geological features while suppressing random noise.

id: Bangliu-基于双输入联合深度学习的地震背-2025
title: 基于双输入联合深度学习的地震背景噪声压制技术及应用
authors: Zhao Bangliu, Wang Wei, Liang Qi, Gao Jianhu, Chang Dekuan, Wang Shujiang
abstract: The paper designs a dual-input deep neural network using pre-first-arrival background noise and data-to-be-denoised as synchronous inputs for joint learning, leveraging noise probability distribution to suppress complex background noise while preserving amplitudes.

id: Yan-基于多道卡尔曼滤波神经网络的无-2024
title: 基于多道卡尔曼滤波神经网络的无监督微地震去噪方法
authors: Zhang Yan, Zhang Yongxue, Wei Zixin, Dong Hongli, Han Fei, Zhang Linjun, Wang Jingzhe
abstract: The paper proposes combining multi-trace Kalman filter with RNN neural network operators for unsupervised microseismic denoising, automatically optimizing Kalman parameters via chain-rule gradient differentiation without requiring labeled training data.

id: 陈-基于无监督蒙特卡洛深度学习框架-2024
title: 基于无监督蒙特卡洛深度学习框架的向量化地震随机噪声衰减
authors: CHEN Gui, , LIU Yang, , , ZHANG Mi
abstract: The paper proposes an unsupervised Monte Carlo deep learning framework with zero-lag cross-correlation regularization for vectorized seismic random noise attenuation, using sliding window technology and Monte Carlo vector selection strategy to accelerate optimization.

id: 王-基于数据增广和cnn的地震随机-2019
title: 基于数据增广和CNN的地震随机噪声压制
authors: WANG YuQing, LU WenKai, LIU JinLin, ZHANG Meng, MIAO YongKang
abstract: The paper proposes random seismic noise attenuation based on data augmentation and CNN, using Gaussian noise augmentation for synthetic data training and a label generation method from unlabeled real noisy data for practical applications.

id: 李-模型约束下的在线字典学习地震弱-2019
title: 模型约束下的在线字典学习地震弱信号去噪方法
authors: LI Yong, ZHANG YiMing, LEI Qin, NIU Cong, ZHOU YuBang, YE YunFei
abstract: The paper proposes model-constrained online dictionary learning for seismic weak signal denoising, combining data-driven and model-driven approaches to construct better dictionaries for denoising under low SNR conditions.

id: 张-双稀疏字典和fista的地震数-2019
title: 双稀疏字典和FISTA的地震数据去噪
authors: ZHANG Liang, , HAN LiGuo, , FANG JinWei, ZHANG Pan, LIU ZhengGuang
abstract: The paper proposes seismic data denoising using double sparsity dictionary and FISTA, combining contourlet transform with data-driven tight frame (DDTF) and using FISTA instead of OMP for sparse coding to improve computational efficiency.

id: 刘-地震随机噪声压缩感知迭代压制方-2021
title: 地震随机噪声压缩感知迭代压制方法
authors: LIU Lu, LIU Yang, LIU Cai, ZHENG ZhiSheng
abstract: The paper proposes an iterative seismic random noise suppression method based on compressive sensing, using iterative soft thresholding and debiasing technology to recover time-space varying coherent axis information from non-stationary random noise.

title: 基于DMFF-Net的地震随机噪声压制新技术
authors: ZHONG Tie, CHEN Yun, LU ShaoPing, DONG XinTong, LI Yue, YANG BaoJun
abstract: The paper proposes DMFF-Net (Double-layer Multi-scale Feature Fusion Denoising Network) for seismic random noise suppression, using multi-branch blocks, dilated convolution, and skip connections to enhance weak signal recovery.

id: 董-基于卷积对抗降噪网络的塔里木盆-2022
title: 基于卷积对抗降噪网络的塔里木盆地沙漠地震资料消噪方法研究
authors: DONG XinTong, ZHONG Tie, WANG HongZhou, WU Ning, LI Yue, YANG BaoJun
abstract: The paper proposes DSCA-Net (Desert Seismic Convolutional Adversarial Denoising Network) for denoising Tarim Basin desert seismic data, using a denoiser combined with MSE loss and adversarial loss to suppress desert background noise.

id: 邵-基于时频分析与分数阶最优控制网-2023
title: 基于时频分析与分数阶最优控制网络的地震数据噪声压制
authors: SHAO Dan, LI TongLin, HAN LiGuo, LI Yue, WU Ning
abstract: The paper proposes TF-FOCNet combining ideal time-frequency analysis with fractional optimal control network for seismic data noise suppression, enhancing low-frequency information retention while attenuating random noise.

title: Low-Frequency Noise Suppression Method Based on Improved DnCNN in Desert Seismic Data
authors: Yuxing Zhao, Yue Li, Xintong Dong, Baojun Yang
abstract: The paper proposes an improved DnCNN for low-frequency noise suppression in desert seismic data, addressing the challenge of severe frequency-band overlap between noise and effective signals in desert environments.

title: Residual Learning of Deep Convolutional Neural Network for Seismic Random Noise Attenuation
authors: Feng Wang, Shengchang Chen
abstract: The paper applies residual learning of deep convolutional neural network for seismic random noise attenuation, learning the mapping from noisy data to noise residual for effective noise suppression.

title: Deep Residual Encoder–Decoder Networks for Desert Seismic Noise Suppression
authors: Ma, Haitao and Yao, Haiyang and Li, Yue and Wang, Hongzhou
abstract: The paper proposes deep residual encoder-decoder networks for desert seismic noise suppression, using residual learning and encoder-decoder architecture to handle low SNR and low-frequency noise characteristics of desert seismic data.

title: Adaptive Dictionary Learning for Blind Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Xiaojing Wang, Jianwei Ma
abstract: The paper proposes adaptive dictionary learning for blind seismic data denoising, learning dictionaries directly from noisy data without requiring clean training data for dictionary construction.

title: A Branch Construction-Based CNN Denoiser for Desert Seismic Data
authors: Lin, Hongbo and Wang, Shifu and Li, Yue
abstract: The paper proposes a branch construction-based CNN denoiser for desert seismic data, designing specialized network branches to handle different frequency components and noise characteristics in desert environments.

title: A Deep-Learning-Based Denoising Method for Multiarea Surface Seismic Data
authors: Dong, Xintong and Zhong, Tie and Li, Yue
abstract: The paper proposes a deep-learning-based denoising method for multiarea surface seismic data, using CNN architecture to handle diverse noise characteristics across different acquisition areas.

title: DCNNs-Based Denoising With a Novel Data Generation for Multidimensional Geological Structures Learning
authors: Sang, Wenjing and Yuan, Sanyi and Yong, Xueshan and Jiao, Xinqi and Wang, Shangxu
abstract: The paper proposes DCNNs-based denoising with novel data generation for multidimensional geological structures learning, generating training data that captures complex geological structures for better denoising generalization.

title: The Denoising of Desert Seismic Data Based on Cycle-GAN With Unpaired Data Training
authors: Li, Yue and Wang, Hongzhou and Dong, Xintong
abstract: The paper proposes denoising desert seismic data using Cycle-GAN with unpaired data training, enabling unsupervised learning when paired clean-noisy data is unavailable for desert seismic processing.

title: A Convolutional Autoencoder Method for Simultaneous Seismic Data Reconstruction and Denoising
authors: J. Jiang, H. Ren, M. Zhang
abstract: The paper proposes a convolutional autoencoder method for simultaneous seismic data reconstruction and denoising, using group convolution and Inception structures to achieve both tasks in a single framework.

title: An Unsupervised Deep Learning Method for Denoising Prestack Random Noise
authors: Liu, Bin and Yue, Jinghang and Zuo, Zhiwu and Xu, Xinji and Fu, Chao and Yang, Senlin and Jiang, Peng
abstract: The paper proposes an unsupervised deep learning method for denoising prestack random noise using a generator CNN with early stopping, exploiting self-similarity of prestack data without requiring clean labels.

title: Deep Learning Prior Model for Unsupervised Seismic Data Random Noise Attenuation
authors: Chenyu Qiu, Bangyu Wu, Naihao Liu, Xu Zhu, Haoran Ren
abstract: The paper proposes a deep learning prior model for unsupervised seismic data random noise attenuation, using deep image prior framework to separate signal from noise without requiring training data or clean labels.

title: DnResNeXt Network for Desert Seismic Data Denoising
authors: H. Yao, H. Ma, Y. Li, Q. Feng
abstract: The paper proposes DnResNeXt network for desert seismic data denoising, using ResNeXt-based architecture with residual learning to predict and subtract desert noise, outperforming traditional methods in field experiments.

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Seismic Data Reconstruction and Denoising
authors: Fanlei Meng, QinYin Fan, Yue Li
abstract: The paper proposes a self-supervised learning approach for seismic data reconstruction and blind denoising requiring no labeled training data, using a masking procedure and modified UNet with two-level nested autoencoder structure.

title: The Application of Semisupervised Attentional Generative Adversarial Networks in Desert Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Yue Li, Xinming Luo, Ning Wu, Xing Dong
abstract: The paper applies semisupervised attentional generative adversarial networks (U-GAT-IT) for desert seismic data denoising, using CAM-guided attention modules and adaptive layer-instance normalization to reduce false reflections.

title: A Self-Supervised Method Using Noise2Noise Strategy for Denoising CRP Gathers
authors: Xiaokai Wang, Siyuan Fan, Chen Zhao, Dawei Liu, Wenchao Chen
abstract: The paper proposes a self-supervised method using Noise2Noise strategy for denoising CRP gathers, requiring only raw seismic data for training without clean labels, effective for both random and coherent noise suppression.

title: Hybrid Shot2Shot and Re-De-Noising Regularization for Random Noise Attenuation of Seismic Data
authors: Aoqi Song, Changpeng Wang, Chunxia Zhang, Jiangshe Zhang, Xiaoli Wei, Deng Xiong
abstract: The paper proposes hybrid Shot2Shot and re-de-noising regularization for random noise attenuation of seismic data, exploiting correlation between neighboring shot-gathers and independence of random noise.

title: RMCHN: A Residual Modular Cascaded Heterogeneous Network for Noise Suppression in DAS-VSP Records
authors: Tie Zhong, Weiyu Wang, Shaoping Lu, Xintong Dong, Baojun Yang
abstract: The paper proposes RMCHN, a residual modular cascaded heterogeneous network for noise suppression in DAS-VSP records, using heterogeneous convolution and long-short path feature learning strategy.

title: DAS Noise Suppression Network Based on Distributing-Local-Attention Expansion
authors: Li, Juan and Xiong, Pan and Li, Yue and Feng, Qiankun
abstract: The paper proposes DAS noise suppression network based on distributing-local-attention expansion (Urefiner), combining CNN preprocessing with refining transformers and DLA module for local and global feature fusion.

title: Enhancing Seismic Data Denoising Through Multiscale Analysis Across Transformer and GAN
authors: Zhang, Junsan and Wang, Wenxue and Li, Kun and Wang, Haoge and Wei, Zhoutuo
abstract: The paper proposes STGAN combining transformer and GAN with multiscale feature extraction for seismic data denoising, capturing temporal dependencies and complex noise features simultaneously.

title: ME-TransNet: A Modal-Enhanced Transformer Denoising Network for Attenuating Low-Frequency Swell Noise
authors: Wang, Shengnan and Lu, Shaoping and Ren, Kai
abstract: The paper proposes ME-TransNet, a modal-enhanced transformer denoising network for attenuating low-frequency swell noise, using VMD sparse sub-layer and SwinIR denoising sub-layer with transformer backbone.

title: Multiscale Residual Convolution Neural Network for Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Gao, Zhimin and Chen, Honglong and Li, Zhe and Ma, Bolun
abstract: The paper proposes a multiscale residual convolution neural network for seismic data denoising, using multiscale feature extraction and residual learning to improve denoising performance.

title: Swin Transformer for Seismic Denoising
authors: F. Li, H. Liu, W. Wang, J. Ma
abstract: The paper proposes Swin Transformer for seismic denoising, leveraging shifted window self-attention and hierarchical feature extraction to capture both local and global waveform characteristics.

title: DIP-MoG: Non-i.i.d. Seismic Noise Attenuation Using Mixture of Gaussians Noise Model and Deep Image Prior
authors: Wang Yuqing, Peng Jiangjun, Wu Bangyu, Li Bo
abstract: The paper proposes DIP-MoG for non-i.i.d. seismic noise attenuation using mixture of Gaussians noise model and deep image prior, addressing spatially varying complex seismic noise without training datasets.

title: Double Dictionary Learning for Seismic Random and Erratic Noise Attenuation
authors: Shekhar, Nakka and Tejaswi, Dokku and Chinnadurai, Sunil and Elumalai, Karthikeyan
abstract: The paper proposes double dictionary learning for seismic random and erratic noise attenuation, using two parallel adaptive dictionaries with regularized K-SVD to reduce computational costs compared to DSDL.

title: Seismic Swell Noise Suppression Using a Wavelet-Transform-Integrated Attention U-Net
authors: Tan, Zhoujie and Yao, Gang and Yuan, Sanyi and Zhang, Feng and Wu, Di
abstract: The paper proposes seismic swell noise suppression using a wavelet-transform-integrated attention U-Net, combining wavelet transform multi-resolution analysis with attention mechanism for marine seismic swell noise.

title: Simultaneous Suppression of Random Noise and Ground Roll With a Fuzzy Logic-Guided Deep Network
authors: Zuo, Xuebin and Ma, Jitao and Liao, Zhen and Chen, Xiaohong
abstract: The paper proposes simultaneous suppression of random noise and ground roll with a fuzzy logic-guided deep network (DASDNet), using STFT, DWT, and fuzzy-logic-based encoder with time-frequency regularization.

title: Tensor Decomposition Dictionary Learning With Low-Rank Regularization for Seismic Data Denoising
authors: Liu, Lina and Liu, Zhao
abstract: The paper proposes tensor decomposition dictionary learning with low-rank regularization for seismic data denoising, decomposing data in front direction and using low rankness of coding coefficients for 3D and 5D data.

title: Unsupervised Seismic Erratic Noise Suppression Using Implicit Neural Representation
authors: Qianzong Bao, Weiwei Xu, Wei Shi, Ji Li, Xiaokai Wang, Wenchao Chen
abstract: The paper proposes unsupervised seismic erratic noise suppression using implicit neural representation with Fourier feature mapping and robust Huber loss, enabling lightweight MLP-based denoising without training pairs.

title: Seismic Denoising via Multiround SCU-Net
authors: Yuli Qi, Guoxin Chen, Jinxin Chen, Jun Li, Rongsen Du, Haiyang Lu, Naijian Wang, Xingguo Huang
abstract: The paper proposes seismic denoising via multiround SCU-Net, using iterative residual-based training with Swin-Transformer Convolution U-Net and SSIM loss for progressive noise suppression while preserving signals.

title: Simultaneous denoising and resolution enhancement of seismic data based on elastic convolution dictionary learning
authors: Nan-Ying Lan, Fan-Chang Zhang, Kai-Heng Sang, Xing-Yao Yin
abstract: The paper proposes simultaneous denoising and resolution enhancement of seismic data based on elastic convolution dictionary learning, using nonconvex elastic L1/2 norm and high-resolution dictionary construction.

title: An EDCC-EMD analysis-based network for DAS VSP data denoising in frequency domain
authors: Huan-Huan Tang, Shi-Jun Cheng, Wu-Qun Li, Wei-Jian Mao
abstract: The paper proposes EDCC-EMD analysis-based network for DAS VSP data denoising in frequency domain, using exponential decay curve constrained EMD for accurate noise label generation and frequency-domain U-Net training.

title: A Self-Supervised Denoising Method Based on Deep Noise Estimation
authors: Lin, Hongbo and Sun, Fuyao
abstract: Seismic random noise attenuation is an essential procedure when processing seismic data. Due to various acquisition environments and complex geological conditions, random noise in seismic field data exhibits spatiotemporal levels, significantly increasing the difficulty of extracting seismic signals. The deep learning (DL) methods have shown excellent performances for seismic denoising. However, most existing discriminative DL methods cannot fit field data with noise levels and signal structures that differ from the training set. To tackle the unmatching challenge, we propose a self-supervised deep denoising model named noise estimation-based convolution neural network (NE-CNN), which contains a multiscale denoising module as a pretrained model and a dual-path noise estimation module that estimates the noise level of each data patch with a generalized Gaussian distribution and gray-level co-occurrence matrix (GLCM). With Stein’s unbiased risk estimate (SURE), we can fine-tune the pretrained denoising module according to the estimated noise levels in a self-supervised style solely on data to be processed, leading to a boost of robustness to nonstationary seismic noise. In synthetic and field data tests, NE-CNN performed satisfactorily compared with discriminative DL methods in denoising effects on seismic data with nonstationary noise.

title: RMCHN: A Residual Modular Cascaded Heterogeneous Network for Noise Suppression in DAS-VSP Records
authors: Tie Zhong, Weiyu Wang, Shaoping Lu, Xintong Dong, Baojun Yang
abstract: The paper proposes RMCHN, a residual modular cascaded heterogeneous network for noise suppression in DAS-VSP records, using heterogeneous convolution and long-short path feature learning strategy.

title: Complex Noise Suppression Using a Robust Dictionary Learning Approach
authors: Feng, Zhenjie
abstract: Seismic data comprise reflection signals and various types of noise, including random high-frequency ambient noise, high-amplitude noise, and low-frequency ground-roll noise. Noise removal while protecting useful signals has been the long-term research focus of seismic exploration and data processing. Seldom methods can be used to deal with all these types of noise simultaneously, which motivates the study of this research. Here, we propose a systematic and effective method to tackle various kinds of complex seismic noise in seismic data by designing a dictionary learning approach. To deal with random and high-amplitude noise, we propose a robust inversion framework to estimate the signals from noise-corrupted data iteratively. The inversion scheme is inspired by an L1 -norm regularized iterative denoising framework but performs conveniently in terms of the coherency-promoting denoising operator. Then, we propose a bandpass-limited signal retrieving scheme internally within the iterative framework to tackle the ground-roll suppression issue. The iterative robust dictionary learning approach is based on a singular-value decomposition (SVD)-free approach to improve the efficiency without sacrificing the denoising performance. Results from synthetic and real data examples show promising performance of the proposed method.

title: Robust Seismic Denoising Framework: A GAN-Based Approach With Multiscale Feature Fusion and Signal Preservation
authors: Li, Kun and Yuan, Jin and Ge, Wei and Yin, Xingyao and Zong, Zhaoyun
abstract: Seismic data acquisition is often affected by complex field conditions and equipment precision limitations, leading to the generation of multiple types of noise that reduce the accuracy of reservoir inversion and geological interpretation. Deep learning techniques have begun to be applied in seismic data denoising due to their efficient feature learning capabilities. However, the complex characteristics of seismic noise pose challenges for conventional convolutional neural networks in learning diverse noise features and achieving effective feature fusion. We propose a novel seismic data denoising methodology based on generative adversarial networks (SD-GAN). The established denoising framework achieves noise distribution prediction through adversarial learning while integrating a deep residual convolutional network (DnCNN) with attention mechanisms to precisely learn diverse noise distributions and enable feature fusion. The DnCNN incorporates deformable convolutions that dynamically adjust convolution kernel shapes to adapt to different seismic signal characteristics, enhancing the network’s modeling capability for complex geological structures. To ensure effective frequency-domain denoising performance, an adaptive frequency-domain loss term is introduced in the loss function to maximize the preservation of seismic signal characteristics. Validation through synthetic seismic data experiments and real seismic data applications demonstrates that this method exhibits excellent robustness and feasibility across various denoising scenarios.

title: Seismic Denoising via Multiround SCU-Net
authors: Yuli Qi, Guoxin Chen, Jinxin Chen, Jun Li, Rongsen Du, Haiyang Lu, Naijian Wang, Xingguo Huang
abstract: The paper proposes seismic denoising via multiround SCU-Net, using iterative residual-based training with Swin-Transformer Convolution U-Net and SSIM loss for progressive noise suppression while preserving signals.

title: A hybrid WUDT-NAFnet for simultaneous source data deblending
authors: Chao-Fan Ke and Shao-Huan Zu and Jun-Xing Cao and Xu-Dong Jiang and Chao Li and Xing-Ye Liu
abstract: Simultaneous source technology, which reduces seismic survey time and improves the quality of seismic data by firing more than one source with a narrow time interval, is compromised by the massive blended interference. Therefore, deblending algorithms have been developed to separate this interference. Recently, deep learning (DL) has been proved its great potential in suppressing the interference. The most popular DL method employs neural network as a filter to attenuate the blended noise in an iterative estimation and subtraction framework (IESF). However, there are still amplitude distortion and blended noise residual problems, especially when dealing with weak signal submerged in strong interference. To address these problems, we propose a hybrid WUDT-NAFnet, which contains two sub-networks. The first network is a wavelet based U-shape deblending transformer network (WUDTnet), incorporated into IESF as a robust regularization term to iteratively separate the blended interference. The second network is a nonlinear activate free network (NAFnet) designed to recover the event amplitude and further suppress the weak noise residual in IESF. With the hybrid network, the blended noise can be separated purposefully and accurately. Examples using synthetic and field seismic data demonstrate that the WUDT-NAFnet outperforms traditional curvelet transform (CT) based method and the deblending transformer (DT) model in terms of deblending. Additionally, for field applications, the data augmentation method of bicubic interpolation is applied to mitigate the feature difference between synthetic and field data. Consequently, the trained network exhibits strong signal preservation ability in numerical field example without requiring additional training.

title: Self-supervised simultaneous deblending and interpolation of incomplete blended data using a multistep blind-trace U-Net
authors: Ben-Feng Wang and Shi-Cong Lin and Xin-Yi Chen
abstract: Blended acquisition offers efficiency improvements over conventional seismic data acquisition, at the cost of introducing blending noise effects. Besides, seismic data often suffers from irregularly missing shots caused by artificial or natural effects during blended acquisition. Therefore, blending noise attenuation and missing shots reconstruction are essential for providing high-quality seismic data for further seismic processing and interpretation. The iterative shrinkage thresholding algorithm can help obtain deblended data based on sparsity assumptions of complete unblended data, and it characterizes seismic data linearly. Supervised learning algorithms can effectively capture the nonlinear relationship between incomplete pseudo-deblended data and complete unblended data. However, the dependence on complete unblended labels limits their practicality in field applications. Consequently, a self-supervised algorithm is presented for simultaneous deblending and interpolation of incomplete blended data, which minimizes the difference between simulated and observed incomplete pseudo-deblended data. The used blind-trace U-Net (BTU-Net) prevents identity mapping during complete unblended data estimation. Furthermore, a multistep process with blending noise simulation-subtraction and missing traces reconstruction-insertion is used in each step to improve the deblending and interpolation performance. Experiments with synthetic and field incomplete blended data demonstrate the effectiveness of the multistep self-supervised BTU-Net algorithm.
